programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Part one of two. Steve and Mark investigate the murder of a mysterious masked magician, who was unpopular with fellow illusionists for revealing some of their most closely guarded professional secrets on TV. Dick Van Dyke stars

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Part two of two. Mark pits his wits against a murderous TV executive who is intent on destroying rival shows on other networks. Medical detective drama, guest starring Ian Ogilvy

programme.title: The Fugitive

programme.title: Perry Mason

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: The CSIs uncover a plot to distribute a new type of ammunition designed to explode on impact, and the ensuing investigation brings Horatio into contact with old adversary Ron Saris - a reunion destined to have deadly consequences. Drama, starring David Caruso, Emily Procter and Adam Rodriguez

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A US Navy lieutenant is murdered at the Defence Logistics Agency, while visitings the office of an admiral currently away at sea. As the team investigates the scene of the crime, they realise a rare painting found in the admiral's office is actually an elaborate fake placed there after the killing, replacing the stolen original. As the quest to locate the missing artwork gets under way, the agents realise they need the help of an expert. Luckily, Tony's con-artist father has returned to town, having broken up with his fiancee, and is on hand to provide expert advice as the hunt for the killer takes them into the shady world of the black market. Guest starring Robert Wagner

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Callen and Sam pose as traffic cops to infiltrate a gang of corrupt officers and military personnel who have been allowing smugglers and criminals a safe passage. Unfortunately, just as they are on the verge of solving the case, the group's leader is shot dead

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Kimble makes a careless error disclosing his whereabouts and Gerard, hastily returning from a vacation with his wife, closes in

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: Shortly after a fortune-teller tells her she has a wedding and a funeral in her future, a woman finds herself accused of murdering her new husband

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: McGee feels the pressure as the team investigates a double murder that appears to be inspired by his still unpublished new novel. His sense of guilt is compounded by the realisation that one of his colleagues could become the killer's third target. American drama following the work of the Naval Criminal Investigative Service, starring Sean Murray and Mark Harmon

programme.desc: A woman is murdered at the home of Gunnery Sergeant Holt Perkins, and while the victim is initially assumed to be the marine's wife, analysis reveals her to be a convicted thief, presumed to be on the scene as part of a plot to hit Perkins' house. When the agents discover the calling card of notorious terror cell Dragon's Tooth on the body, it becomes clear the murder has been committed as part of a wider agenda, and the team joins forces with the Department of Defence to catch the killer. American drama, starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Cote de Pablo and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Chief petty officer Leland Wiley dies of a heart attack just as he is about to reveal information on elusive arms dealer Agah Bayar. The team discovers the cardiac arrest was caused by someone hacking into a computer monitoring Wiley's pacemaker and increasing his heart rate to 400bpm. Meanwhile, a probationary agent tries to impress Gibbs and win a place on his team. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon, with Tamer Hassan and Matt L Jones

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Confidential files are leaked by an anonymous hacker, who threatens to release more unless the Navy agrees to renew a contract with a struggling shipbuilding firm. The Navy Secretary suspects the company's owner Philip Wickes is to blame, and asks NCIS to join forces with the PsyOps unit in an elaborate ruse to bring the culprit out of hiding. Meanwhile, Gibbs' colleagues begin to notice his attraction to his opposite number Samantha Ryan. Guest starring Jamie Lee Curtis

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Vance's brother-in-law Michael Thomas is linked to the murder of a petty officer, and Gibbs is placed under pressure to clear the man's name. Meanwhile, a negative performance review leaves Tony feeling downhearted and he resolves to prove the assessment wrong by being less chatty while on duty. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Rocky Carroll

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A marine lieutenant's body falls from the sky and the team links the man's death to the disappearance of a Navy chaplain thought to have been kidnapped by rebels working with Colombian crime cartels. Ziva and Tony are dispatched to conduct a rescue operation and enlist the help of former Interpol agent Monique Lisson, while Gibbs discovers there was more to the chaplain's outreach mission than meets the eye. Crime drama, guest starring Karina Lombard (The L Word) and Jamie Lee Curtis (A Fish Called Wanda)

programme.desc: A fire crew discovers a man's body after putting out a raging blaze at a warehouse belonging to a Navy-contracted electronics company, and Gibbs recognises the victim as Carter Plimpton - a former member of an NCIS black-ops unit. programme.desc: The duo are called in to find the murderer of an eight-year-old girl, a case which dredges up disturbing memories for Dalziel, who investigated a similar incident in the same area 15 years earlier. The townsfolk are sure a missing local man is guilty of both crimes, but new evidence soon points toward a more complex solution. Feature-length detective drama, starring Warren Clarke, Colin Buchanan, Joanna David, Ronald Pickup and Kaye Wragg

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The arrest of a Turkish girl who has been working illegally leads Ucos to reopen the case of Richard Gibson, a pub landlord who died in a fire in 2009. The original investigation concluded Gibson's death was probably suicide due to his dire financial situation, but the detectives have some questions about the pub's live-in barmaid, who disappeared on the night of the blaze. Could widowed landlady Joanne, pub regular Jason, or the dead man's best friend David, who happens to have a sideline in passing off his British-produced sparkling wine as French champagne, supply them with any answers? While the case tests the team's brain power, Sasha is also keen to raise their fitness levels as she signs them up for a five-a-side football competition. Phil Davis, Niamh Cusack and Tom Georgeson guest star

However, when more remains are discovered, it becomes clear they are looking for a serial killer - and news of the crimes preys deeply on the mind of a young mother who is harbouring a dark secret. Emilia Fox and David Caves star, with appearances from Martin Compston (Line of Duty) and Gary Lewis (Billy Elliot)

programme.desc: The sleuth is called upon to mediate in a dispute at a condemned block of flats, and soon discovers the man responsible for their construction has a shady past. Drama, guest starring former Coronation Street actresses Eva Pope and Georgia Taylor, with Philip Glenister and John Simm

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Steve McAndrew brings his dying father to London so he can spend his final days in a nearby hospice, but how much time he can spend with his parent is anyone's guess - the Ucos team is hard at work investigating the cold case murder of 55-year-old interpreter Agnes Bradley. A DNA sample provides a link to a rebellious teenager, and the team wonders if the killer could be related to him. But a whole new can of worms is opened when investigators discover the lad's mother was raped - could the same person have killed Agnes? Gerry and Steve hope that interviewing her friends from a local chess club may shed new light on the matter, but it's a possible professional issue that could hold the key to unlocking the mystery. Denis Lawson and Dennis Waterman star

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. A homeless man opens an abandoned suitcase in a park, only to find the body of a teenage girl inside. Jack and Nikki attend the scene with DS Sally Kirchner and are shocked to discover the victim's baby has been cut from her womb. From the placenta they are able to identify the father - but the married man in question denies any knowledge of the deceased. Emilia Fox, David Caves and Richard Lintern star in the forensic crime drama, with guest appearances by Morven Christie and Sean Gallagher

programme.title: Frontline Fightback
programme.desc: How a brave mother fought back against car thieves as they tried to steal her car from her driveway - with her baby inside. Narrated by Rav Wilding

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: Special Operations Paramedics Mark and Leigh are called to a car crash in Livingston, where a patient needs to be fully immobilised after complaining of neck pain. Paramedic Gary and Technician Pete attend a patient in Barrhead who has been coughing up blood and needs urgent assessment. Meanwhile, in Edinburgh, Rapid Response Paramedic John is called to a patient with back pain after a fall who is reluctant to go to hospital

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp is joined by experts Danny Sebastian and David Harper at Nottingham racecourse as two teams are challenged to scour an antiques fair and pick up bargains to sell for profit at auction. Natasha also goes behind the scenes at the National Justice Museum, which is based in Nottingham

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Al reluctantly leads a shared patient consultation, while Bear may be out of his depth. The connection between a radio host, a graphic novelist and a timid man needs to be determined

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: After long and demanding careers in the emergency services, a hitherto busy couple are after a change of scenery in the Norfolk countryside. Jonnie Irwin guides them on their rural property tour, showing them some homes available for their £900,000 budget. Jonnie also pays a visit to 15th-century manor house Oxburgh Hall and learns the tricks of the trade used for its 21st-century restoration

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Glaswegian friends Andrew and Stephen have very different experiences of cooking - Andrew cooks mostly for his family and is hoping to get a bit more experimental, whereas Stephen loves to throw dinner parties for friends. Rylan Clark-Neal presides as the two compete, with chefs Romy Gill and Ellis Barrie helping the pair pull out all the stops to create some amazing dishes

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Steve Fletcher and Dominic Chinea tackle the motor and body of a hand-made toy racing car, while leather expert Suzie Fletcher get to grips with a leather bowls bag in need of restoration to pass onto the fifth generation of a sports-mad family. Wood expert Will Kirk works his magic on an intricately carved box from the Far East. Its owner's mother bought it with her when she came to Europe from Singapore as a young bride

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas host the magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: With her hearing looming, Stacey has a fraught final showdown with Martin, while Jean reassures her daughter she will take care of everything at home. Kathy and Kim question Bobby as he waits for his second date, fully embracing his more confident new persona. Mick learns that other survivors have come forward, hopeful that they will be able to give evidence instead of him

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: The business ideas contest moves to BBC One, with entrepreneurs Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies eager to strike more potentially lucrative deals. In this first episode, a pair of tea entrepreneurs find themselves in hot water when their numbers are interrogated, the Dragons discover hidden artistic talents when a mindfulness art business is pitched, an optical inventor hopes to have investment in sight with his anti-glare eyewear and a husband-and-wife team have the needs of NHS workers at the heart of their skin-care business. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The semi-finals continue as the second group of surviving amateurs cook a dish for John Torode and Gregg Wallace that they would have on the menu of their own restaurant, pop-up or featured in their cookbook. In an unexpected twist, the contestants discover that they are also presenting their food to top chef Marcus Wareing. After two leave the contest, the remaining three head to The Game Bird restaurant at The Stafford Hotel to work with culinary director Ben Tish, serving the restaurant's staff. No one knows the food better than they do and expectations are high of the contenders

programme.title: The Accountant (2016)
programme.desc: High-functioning autistic number-cruncher Christian Wolff moonlights as a money launderer for the mob. However, the Treasury Department start closing in on him, so he decides to take on a legitimate client in the form of a robotics company. As he works through their books, he uncovers a discrepancy involving millions of dollars. Thriller, starring Ben Affleck, Anna Kendrick and JK Simmons

programme.desc: Naga Munchetty and Charlie Stayt present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Frontline Fightback
programme.desc: Rav Wilding recounts the brutal attack of a 96-year-old D-Day war veteran on his doorstep. Police in Taunton, Somerset, quickly arrested his attacker after tracking his spending habits

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: Special Operations Paramedics Dan and Leigh are called to assist bomb disposal experts after a hazardous chemical is found at a building site. Lee and Nadine race to the scene of a three-vehicle smash on icy roads in Melrose, while paramedics in Oban make two trips to visit a regular patient whose breathing is deteriorating

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Caroline Hawley and Danny Sebastian go antique hunting with two teams at the Royal Cheshire Showground. Presenter Eric Knowles observes proceedings and also discovers the story of two extraordinary Victorian women who brought ancient Egypt to Macclesfield

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The amateur sleuth struggles to protect those closest to him when an old foe is unexpectedly released from prison. Mark Williams stars

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin is in Pembrokeshire with a couple who want not only to buy their first house together, but also need accommodation for holidaymakers and a horse

programme.title: Heavenly Gardens with Alexander Armstrong
programme.desc: The first of two programmes in which the presenter and garden designer Arit Anderson visit gardens of reflection and contemplation. At the remote Pluscarden Abbey in north-east Scotland, Alexander helps harvest luscious apples and accompanies the monks singing midday psalms. Meanwhile, Arit walks in the footsteps of royalty in the glorious grounds of Sudeley Castle. Plus, Alexander returns to his student haunts in Cambridge to discover a place designed to inspire joy, even on the bleakest days, in the university's Botanic Garden

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: A beloved stoneware elephant's fall from a mantlepiece left its sentimental owner shattered into tiny pieces along with the ornament, so ceramics expert Kirsten Ramsay sets about performing a delicate restoration to revive both Michel's precious item and her memories. Plus, Will Kirk and Suzie Fletcher join forces to resurrect a dilapidated child's rocking horse and Brenton West undertakes the repair of an unusual trophy shaped like a boxing ring

programme.title: The Sound of Music (1965)
programme.desc: A cheerful novice nun is employed as governess to the seven children of an austere widower. As she brings light, laughter and a love of all things harmonious to a family previously beset by grief and strict discipline she also falls in love with her employer. But before long, the looming threat of the Second World War jeopardises their happy household. Oscar-winning Rodgers and Hammerstein musical, starring Julie Andrews, Christopher Plummer and Eleanor Parker

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Sharon heads to the gym with the estate agent, where Ben, keen to scupper her plans, makes his own offer on the business. Lily asks Jean if she and the other kids can have an afternoon tea party - but when their gran goes off to buy supplies, the scheming youngster packs a suitcase. Callum's wedding plans are causing problems and embarrassment

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The last challenge of the semi-finals sees the six remaining amateurs cook-off to win a coveted place in the MasterChef Finals. They are tasked to each cook an outstanding dish to impress revered restaurant critics Grace Dent, Jay Rayner and Jimi Famurewa. Serving food to these masters of the dining room is a daunting task for the most adept of chefs, and these talented home-cooks now have to put their skills and palates on the line. Any mistake could cost them the competition, so the pressure is firmly on. It remains to be seen who John Torode and Gregg Wallace will take through to the prestigious finals week

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon presents a special edition looking back over the best moments from the series. The celebrity panellists joining team captains Lee Mack and David Mitchell include Jo Brand, Daisy May Cooper, Stacey Dooley, Ronan Keating, Prue Leith, Scarlett Moffatt, Diane Morgan and Bob Mortimer

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by Octavia Spencer and Melissa McCarthy, who discuss working together in superhero comedy Thunder Force, while David Schwimmer and Nick Mohammed - co-stars of spy series Intelligence - also appear. Comedian Frank Skinner and singer and star of RuPaul's Drag Race Michelle Visage are also on Graham's guest roster. Plus, Steps perform Heartbreak in This City

programme.title: Spooks: The Greater Good (2015)
programme.desc: A terrorist escapes custody during a prisoner handover when the convoy he is being transported in is attacked, leading to a CIA operative being killed and relations becoming frayed between British and American intelligence agencies. MI5 officer Will Holloway must team up with disgraced intelligence chief Harry Pearce to track him down before an imminent terrorist attack on London. Spy thriller, starring Kit Harington and Peter Firth

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Gennaro Contaldo and Shivi Ramoutar, plus special guest Mel Giedroyc. Olly Smith joins via video link to pick wine to go with the studio dishes

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Four friends take on a terraced house in Crewe. A former shop in a Cumbrian village and a three-bedroom terraced house in Gnosall in Staffordshire are also refurbished

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's top-flight fixtures, including Leicester City v Manchester City and Manchester United v Brighton & Hove Albion

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: A mother and son take on a mother and daughter at the Royal Highland Centre in Edinburgh. Paul Laidlaw and John Cameron help the teams while Anita Manning leads proceedings

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal oversees the action as sisters Elsa and Gabriella pair up with chefs Jeremy Pang and Akis Petretzikis to prepare a meal in 20 minutes

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a couple who have £500,000 to spend on a large family home in Northern Ireland where they can also keep animals and welcome paying guests. Away from the search, Nicki immerses herself in the country's maritime history at the dry dock where RMS Titanic was built

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore and her team transforms a filing cabinet, petrol can, table and chest of drawers from tips in Greater Manchester and Surrey in the hope of making a profit

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Anu and Ash live in Coventry with their cat Quinn, where their garden is currently a new build blank canvas divided into two cramped tiers. The couple want to turn it into a pet-friendly delight, inspired by the gardens and countryside they fell in love with on holidays in Tuscany, Italy. They want lots of interest for Quinn and a large inviting patio where they can entertain their friends and family. Arit Anderson and Charlie Dimmock have a £3,500 budget to make the couple's dreams come true

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys invites Steve Backshall, Noreen Khan, Rhys James and Caroline Flint to take to the black chair to be put to the test on their chosen specialist subjects, which are sharks, Marilyn Monroe, The US remake of The Office and classic horror movie Alien. The second round sees them tackling general knowledge questions in a bid to carry off the commemorative trophy

programme.title: Catchpoint
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts a new family-friendly game show combining physical fun and an exciting question mechanic, as contestants have to think fast as they answer questions while trying to catch balls of various sizes dropped from the studio ceiling, with a variety of entertaining rounds and a cash prize at stake

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: A married couple from Merthyr Tydfil answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Hosted by Danny Dyer

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a special edition of the quiz featuring teams of comedians. Rosie Jones, Miles Jupp, Russell Kane, Lucy Beaumont, Rhys James, Lloyd Griffith, Phil Wang and Sindhu Vee try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Ollie nearly chokes to death while on a walk in the woods with Grace, forcing her to perform a makeshift surgical procedure using what she has to hand to save his life - an action that could have devastating consequences for both him and her relationship with Connie. Rash discovers that his dad has been lying about his relationship, and Jade sees past her suspicions of Tina

programme.title: Keeping Faith
programme.desc: Rose comes back into Faith's life for the first time in 25 years, claiming to want to clear the air before she dies. However, Faith is not sure she can believe or trust her. Osian's case has its first hearing in court, and Judge Alwen Owens agrees to meet up with him to try to understand what he wants. Steve is racked with guilt over his failure to provide financial stability for his children, and Evan begins spying on Faith

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, including Leicester City v Manchester City and Chelsea v West Bromwich Albion. The Foxes and the Blues were looking for three points to boost their chances of finishing in the top four, but Leicester faced the toughest possible test against the champions elect. Plus, Arsenal v Liverpool and Leeds United v Sheffield United

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker, Alan Shearer and Micah Richards count down the top 10 Premier League hard men, featuring the likes of Roy Keane, Vinnie Jones, Patrick Vieira and Duncan Ferguson

programme.title: Bobby (2016)
programme.desc: Documentary celebrating Bobby Moore, exploring his football career, including leading the England team to victory in the 1966 world cup, and the personal challenges he faced both on the pitch and in retirement. Featuring archive footage and contributions from family, friends and celebrity fans

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Frontline Fightback
programme.desc: After an elderly couple's home is broken into, a police officer looks at burglary patterns in the area and predicts where and when the culprits are likely to strike again. Only this time, he's waiting for them

programme.title: Chris Packham's Animal Einsteins
programme.desc: The presenter introduces animals that build structures and adapt the environment for their own benefit, creating safe places to sleep and raise young or traps to catch prey. The programme examines how the huge dams built by beavers can completely transform the surrounding landscape, the function of the different types of silk spiders are capable of producing, and how leafcutter ants build underground gardens where they farm their own food

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: Three semi-detached properties in Leicester - including a shabby chic family home, and an Edwardian property with some rather eclectic interiors - are in the spotlight

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: The judges - a group of knowledgeable property-obsessed 'nosey neighbours' - return to Leicester to give the winning homes from each category a second look, getting a fresh insight into the houses before deciding which one they like the best. Narrated by Marcus Garvey

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: Woody and George's secret operation is now a team effort, including Shaun, who thinks he has invented the perfect cover for a trip to Australia without Tracy finding out the truth

programme.title: Nature's Great Events
programme.desc: How the growth of phytoplankton along the coastal waters of Alaska and British Columbia attracts a vast array of marine life to feast on billions of herring. Footage includes humpback whales migrating from Hawaii as well as Steller sea lions, dolphins and killer whales that make the long and arduous journey toward the abundant fish, risking malnutrition and attacks from predators along the way. Narrated by David Attenborough

programme.title: The Wonder of Animals
programme.desc: Chris Packham investigates what enables birds of prey to rule the aerial roost, and reveals that their ability to dominate their fellow birds in terms of strength, manoeuvrability and phenomenal speed is down to a combination of anatomical and physiological adaptations. He explains the inner workings of the bald eagle's ratchet talons, and how sharp eyes and a gyroscopic head enable the goshawk to keep its sight firmly fixed on both its prey and its surroundings

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antiques experts Claire Rawle and Adam Partridge appraise collectables that reflect local Kent character at Chiddingstone Castle. Meanwhile, presenter Paul Martin visits Anne Boleyn's family home of Hever Castle to reveal what influenced her rise to power

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley, Matt Forde, Fred MacAulay and Judy Murray test their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday

programme.title: Saved and Remade
programme.desc: A post box used in a wedding ceremony and a worn out but much-loved armchair are among the items transformed into beautiful and functional items by the skilled team of remakers

programme.title: Hairy Bikers: Route 66
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King mount their bikes for the final leg of their epic journey along the American road. They begin in Las Vegas, where they learn how to make apparently the best mashed potato in the world - inspiring them to cook a massive lobster and prawn cocktail. Back on the road they finally reach California, where they follow the path of the 49ers during the gold rush and learn about cooking with marijuana - which is perfectly legal in Los Angeles. They also enjoy the best tacos they have ever eaten at a taco festival, before reaching Santa Monica pier - the end of their trip

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The three chefs are halfway through their heat and the pressure is mounting. Each wants to represent Scotland at a banquet celebrating British invention and innovation. For mains, one chef honours Logie Baird's invention of television with an upmarket take on a TV dinner, and another celebrates Scotland's successful cloning of Dolly The Sheep. A third cooks an ambitious multi-layered pie of beef and chicken with a beef consomme. Presenter Andi Oliver also asks the chefs to prepare a pre-dessert or palate cleanser. Only two will proceed to Friday's judging and have a chance to represent Scotland at the regional finals

programme.title: Pandemic 2020
programme.desc: The story of the first year of the pandemic told by people from all over the world. It begins on New Year's Eve 2019, when huge crowds gathered to celebrate. Contributors include Xie, a young food blogger from Wuhan whose wedding took place a few days before her city went into lockdown. As the virus travels to Europe, Dr Amie from Coventry recalls watching in disbelief the horrific scenes of people dying in Italy

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Extended edition of the comedy panel game. Sandi Toksvig talks about rocks, rolls and rock 'n' roll, with Bill Bailey, Katy Brand, Eshaan Akbar and Alan Davies

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Brexitcast
programme.desc: A mix of light-hearted gossip and serious analysis about the latest goings on from Westminster and Brussels, with Laura Kuenssberg, Katya Adler, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason

programme.title: The Detectives: Fighting Organised Crime
programme.desc: When a man is gunned down on a busy residential street in Manchester, a team of specialist GMP detectives race to identify the masked men responsible

programme.title: DNA Family Secrets
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley helps people answer life-changing questions by examining their DNA. This time, triplets Peter, David and Phillip, who were adopted as young boys, have their DNA analysed by Professor Turi King as they attempt to explore their birth father's heritage. Meanwhile, a woman who was conceived from a sperm donor longs to learn about the other half of her ancestry, and find out if she has any half donor siblings

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The third quarter-final sees four talented heat-winners return to vie for a place in knockout week by participating in the Critic's Test. The challenge - set by infamously hard task-master Jay Rayner - is to prepare a dessert where stone fruit is the star. Gregg Wallace and John Torode preside as the contenders endeavour to take another step closer to being crowned MasterChef Champion 2021

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Simon Parfett and the team travel to Wiltshire to meet Anthony and Bianca who have grand plans to turn the shed at the bottom of their garden into a unique B&B

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Frontline Fightback
programme.desc: How a brave mother fought back against car thieves as they tried to steal her car from her driveway - with her baby inside. Narrated by Rav Wilding

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit Forty Hall in London. Treasures include a pair of hands crafted by Lucian Freud, a destination board from the front of an early London bus, and a model of Donald Campbell's Bluebird, which broke the land-speed record in 1964. There is also a rare set of wedding jewellery from Nepal, and a diamond cross and gold necklace with a hefty price tag

programme.title: Painting the Holy Land
programme.desc: Part one of two. Artist Lachlan Goudie follows Jesus's last days on Earth, the events of Holy Week that see him travel from the north of what is now Israel to Jerusalem and the site of the Crucifixion. It's a pilgrimage that millions undertake, and a story of love and suffering that has inspired some of the world's most remarkable masterpieces

programme.title: Painting the Holy Land
programme.desc: Lachlan Goudie explores the role of Mary in the Bible, and why she has become one of the figures in Christianity most heavily represented in art

programme.title: Britain's Easter Story
programme.desc: In the first of two programmes, choirmaster Gareth Malone and gospel choir conductor Karen Gibson travel round the UK to uncover the stories, music and traditions that have marked the Easter Christian festivals both in the past and today. From the island of Lindisfarne to a Welsh mountain with a unique connection to Good Friday, Gareth finds out what links Easter to some of the country's most stunning historic sites. Meanwhile, Karen unravels the origins of one of the best-known Good Friday hymns and revisits some of the traditions she grew up with

programme.title: King of Kings (1961)
programme.desc: Biblical epic chronicling the life of Christ, from the rumours of a coming Messiah before his birth, through his rise as a spiritual leader to his eventual betrayal, crucifixion and resurrection. Narrated by Orson Welles and starring Jeffrey Hunter, Siobhan McKenna, Robert Ryan, Rip Torn, Hurd Hatfield and Harry Guardino

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A look at TV appearances made over the years by Mary Poppins actress Julie Andrews, capturing the milestones of her life and career. Featuring interviews from the archives as well as classic clips. Narrated by Sylvia Syms

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: This time, Flog It! comes from the RAF Museum in Cosford, Shropshire. Antiques experts Adam Partridge and Claire Rawle are on hand to find a selection of objects to take to auction. Claire gets sentimental over a music box and Adam discovers a silver cigarette case from Russia. Paul Martin falls for an iconic bike and visits the nearby historic Ironbridge to investigate the start of the industrial revolution

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley, Matt Forde, Fred MacAulay and Judy Murray test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games, with the week's overall champion set to be crowned

programme.title: Saved and Remade
programme.desc: The workshop is full of history and memories as a stunning Polish cloak and a collection of family rings are among the remakers' transformations. Sabrina Grant hosts

programme.title: MOTDx
programme.desc: Jermaine Jenas and the team take an offbeat look at the Premier League, also discussing skills, celebrities, fashion, music and culture

programme.title: Pilgrimage: The Road to Istanbul
programme.desc: Adrian Chiles, Edwina Currie, Fatima Whitbread, Mim Shaikh, Amar Latif, Dom Joly and Pauline McLynn still have 650km to travel to reach Istanbul. The last leg of their route takes them through more Bulgarian forest in the Rhodope Mountains, involving a tricky 1,500m climb. Near the summit, the incline increases, though the breath-taking view proves worth it. In Batak, they visit the small orthodox church of St Nedelya where around 5,000 people were massacred by Ottoman militia in the 19th century. Eventually, they arrive in Turkey, heading for the magnificent Suleymaniye Mosque in Istanbul

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: With delicious dishes celebrating Scotland's landmark inventions, who will be judged the winner for Scotland? The judges, including bestselling author Caroline Criado-Perez, must decide. Andi Oliver hosts

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: It's the Easter weekend, which means extra time to enjoy the garden. Monty Don plants out sweet peas for summer colour and gives advice on what to use for mulching borders. Plus, Carol Klein is at RHS Rosemoor to revel in the floral spring sensations that epitomise the season

programme.title: Colette (2018)
programme.desc: In the late 19th century, a woman is pushed by her husband to write novels under his name, but upon their success she fights to make her talents known. Drama, starring Keira Knightley, Fiona Shaw and Dominic West

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Lee Adama leads the Pegasus's desperate bid for survival in the aftermath of the Cylons' siege of the Twelve Colonies. His new commanding officer Major Kendra Shaw is forced to cast away nightmares of her service under Admiral Helen Cain to become a human weapon - known as a razor. First of a two-part standalone story parallelling past events in the series, starring Jamie Bamber and Stephanie Chaves-Jacobsen

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: A desperate rescue mission from Pegasus requires Kendra and Starbuck co-operate, but while one must face her past, the other is told of her future. Concluding the standalone story, starring Katee Sackhoff, Stephanie Chaves-Jacobsen and Jamie Bamber

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: An undercover investigation inside a lab analysing thousands of Covid-19 tests a day. Secretly filmed footage reveals a failing service with shoddy practices where staff complain they are under pressure to meet targets despite the lab often running well below capacity. The programme discovers there have been three outbreaks of coronavirus among staff and that social distancing is poorly maintained. Test samples sometimes arrive poorly packaged and labelled, with equipment frequently malfunctioning, leading to contamination of results. The programme also discovers that tests, including some intended to find new variants of coronavirus, have been wrongly discarded or lost

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: A woman who was drawn into a psychologically abusive relationship and conned into handing over more than £60,000 of her life savings

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Marrying Mum and Dad
programme.desc: Four sisters bring the African plains to life in the form of a 'wild wedding' complete with a cake that fires chocolate into the sky, and a large model elephant

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: A 10-year-old girl with a Turkish dad and an English mum seeks a way to share her heritage with her friends by asking to team to build a Turkish-themed den at her home in Leeds

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: A special green campaign is launched from the BP garden

programme.title: The Amelia Gething Complex
programme.desc: The return of the comedy, starring Amelia Gething. Amelia and her flatmates buy a super-smart TV that leads them into infinite parallel universes - with hilariously disastrous consequences

programme.title: The Amelia Gething Complex
programme.desc: Amelia is elected prime minister and imposes a system of malevolent and despotic rule

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall dives with hammerhead and tiger sharks, exploring how to save them from extinction

programme.title: Richard Hammond's Invisible Worlds
programme.desc: Richard concludes his investigation, revealing a miniature universe of activity lying just beyond the limits of the human eye. He learns how the gecko lizard is capable of defying gravity, and demonstrates how microscopic changes in ice crystals can cause devastating avalanches. Plus, he explores the infectious range of a sneeze

programme.title: Me and My Dog: The Ultimate Contest
programme.desc: Eight dog owners and their pets compete together in a series of physical and mental challenges in the Lake District, in a bid to make it to the grand final. First, the Yellow team have a chance to score points in scent-based orienteering, while the Red team take on a woodland obstacle course. In the final round, the pairs take part in an open-water swim before the scores are revealed

programme.title: Nature's Microworlds
programme.desc: Documentaries exploring ecosystems around the world, with the first edition examining the Galapagos Islands, one of the most famous archipelagos on Earth. The film investigates the reasons the bizarre and unique creatures that live there have managed to thrive and how they inspired Charles Darwin's ideas that would revolutionise biology. Narrated by Steve Backshall

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Moving into the Scotland heats, chefs Roberta Hall, Amy Elles, Stuart Ralson and Scott Smith present their canapes, starters and fish dishes inspired by British invention and innovation. Veteran chef and Michelin-starred Tom Brown, a seafood specialist, tastes their canapes, which range from a cheese tart to a duck liver parfait

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The three chefs are halfway through their heat and the pressure is mounting. Each wants to represent Scotland at a banquet celebrating British invention and innovation. For mains, one chef honours Logie Baird's invention of television with an upmarket take on a TV dinner, and another celebrates Scotland's successful cloning of Dolly The Sheep. A third cooks an ambitious multi-layered pie of beef and chicken with a beef consomme. Presenter Andi Oliver also asks the chefs to prepare a pre-dessert or palate cleanser. Only two will proceed to Friday's judging and have a chance to represent Scotland at the regional finals

programme.title: Nigel Slater's Dish of the Day
programme.desc: The food writer turns the contents of his weekly shop into dishes including ricotta beefburger, smoked mackerel coleslaw, and lemon and thyme cake

programme.title: Death on the Nile (1978)
programme.desc: Detective Hercule Poirot investigates a murder on board a paddle steamer in Egypt. The victim was an unpopular heiress, whose outspoken manner put her at odds with most of her fellow passengers during their cruise on the Nile. Agatha Christie mystery, starring Peter Ustinov, Bette Davis, Maggie Smith, George Kennedy, Mia Farrow, Jane Birkin, Olivia Hussey, Angela Lansbury and David Niven

programme.title: Evil Under the Sun (1982)
programme.desc: Hercule Poirot braves the heat and sand to investigate the murder of a glamorous stage star on an idyllic Adriatic holiday island. It becomes clear many people had reason to want the temperamental actress dead and the Belgian sleuth is soon hard at work figuring out which of her enemies eventually managed to kill her. Mystery, with Peter Ustinov, Diana Rigg, James Mason, Maggie Smith and Jane Birkin

programme.title: Handel's Messiah from English National Opera
programme.desc: Petroc Trelawny and YolanDa Brown present a performance of the oratorio staged at the London Coliseum, and filmed specifically for a television audience. The ENO Chorus and Orchestra is joined by celebrated soloists including countertenor Iestyn Davies, mezzo Christine Rice and soprano Nadine Benjamin, and rising stars Anthony Gregory, Nardus Williams, John Findon, William Thomas and Benson Wilson, while the conductor is Laurence Cummings.

programme.title: Easter from King's
programme.desc: Music and readings for Holy Week with the choristers of the Choir of King's College, Cambridge, and the King's Singers, directed by Daniel Hyde. Well-loved choral music for the season includes Ubi Caritas, God So Loved the World, When I Survey the Wondrous Cross and This Joyful Eastertide, while the story of Jesus' death and Resurrection is told in the words of the King James Bible, read by members of King's College

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: A nostalgic look back at the early part of the decade as the music show embraced the advent of 'new pop', by changing its theme tune and titles and parting company with dance act Legs & Co. Acts featured include Duran Duran, Depeche Mode, the Human League, Kim Wilde, Midge Ure, Jerry Dammers (of the Specials), Beggar and Co, Leee John and Freeez. With Richard Skinner, Mike Read and Carrie Grant

programme.title: A Life in Ten Pictures
programme.desc: Documentary taking a look at a celebrity's life through 10 defining photos, with the star's secrets revealed by those who were there, and those who knew them best. The first edition focuses on Queen frontman Freddie Mercury, beginning with the earliest photo of the singer as a baby, and ending with a private moment captured shortly before his death

programme.title: Queen Rock Montreal
programme.desc: The rock band perform for an audience of more than 18,000 at the Montreal Forum in November 1981. As well as featuring hits including We Will Rock You, Killer Queen and Bohemian Rhapsody, the concert also saw the first live performance of Under Pressure

programme.title: The Story of Bohemian Rhapsody
programme.desc: Richard E Grant narrates the story of the classic Queen track, with band members Brian May and Roger Taylor returning to Rockfield studios to discuss how the music and lyrics came together, while friends and family as well as Abba's Bjorn Ulvaeus and the Darkness reveal their thoughts on the song. Featuring rare footage of Freddie Mercury performing in the studio and the group's first-ever TV appearance

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: The year 1981 embraced disco and ska, new wave punk, the burgeoning New Romantic scene and the rise of synthpop, with some prog quirkiness and good old rock'n'roll thrown in. Performances from big hitter soloists Phil Collins, Shakin' Stevens and Kim Wilde are featured alongside the exuberant chaos of groups like Tenpole Tudor, Adam and the Ants and the Teardrop Explodes. Plus, Odyssey, Clare Grogan, the Specials and the Human League

programme.title: A Late Quartet (2012)
programme.desc: The oldest member of a string quartet announces he is retiring due to ill health. His decision leads to hidden resentments among the others erupting, threatening to destroy years of friendship and artistry. Meanwhile, the teenage daughter of two of the musicians harbours secret desires for one of their colleagues. Drama, starring Christopher Walken, Philip Seymour Hoffman and Catherine Keener

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wild
programme.desc: Dean Burman was a painter-decorator, but his dream was to be a wildlife cameraman. This film shares his amateur footage of pike in Leicester, which he collected over many years

programme.title: The Lakes with Paul Rose
programme.desc: Remote Eskdale is the next destination in the explorer's trek through the Lake District, enjoying the landscapes and meeting its people. The corner of the western Lakes he focuses on is off the normal tourist track, yet boasts England's highest mountain. However, before making the tough climb to the summit of Scafell Pike, Paul meets the folk who run one of the country's most scenic railways, has a close encounter with a vulture, and gets to grips with the locals in a spot of Cumberland wrestling

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a distant waterfall and a bubbling stream against the backdrop of a tumultuous sky

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James addresses the misconceptions of an awkward farmer who prefers to treat his own animals, and Siegfried faces a dilemma as James and Helen's honeymoon looms. A chance encounter with old friend Stewie Brannon ruins Siegfried's chances of landing a plum job with the county horse racing board

programme.title: The Third Man (1949)
programme.desc: American writer Holly Martins goes to post-Second World War Vienna at the invitation of his old friend Harry Lime, only to find he has died. However, the author finds the circumstances surrounding his death fail to add up and goes in search of the truth - but is unprepared for the ugly secrets he uncovers. Carol Reed's thriller, written by Graham Greene, starring Joseph Cotten, Orson Welles, Alida Valli and Trevor Howard

programme.title: Brighton Rock (2010)
programme.desc: A young criminal in 1960s Brighton is caught on camera killing a man. The evidence falls into the hands of a naive waitress, so the icy murderer seduces her in a bid to ensure her silence. Crime drama based on Graham Greene's novel, starring Sam Riley, Andrea Riseborough, Helen Mirren, Andy Serkis and John Hurt

programme.title: Around the World in 80 Treasures
programme.desc: Dan Cruickshank visits Mali and Egypt, where he examines masks said to connect the land of the living with that of the dead, admires the paintings of the Dogon, and learns the story of reputedly the most beautiful woman in history. He also explores the Great Pyramid, then journeys across the desert of Libya before concluding this leg of the expedition with a trip down the Nile

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a distant waterfall and a bubbling stream against the backdrop of a tumultuous sky

programme.title: The Lakes with Paul Rose
programme.desc: Remote Eskdale is the next destination in the explorer's trek through the Lake District, enjoying the landscapes and meeting its people. The corner of the western Lakes he focuses on is off the normal tourist track, yet boasts England's highest mountain. However, before making the tough climb to the summit of Scafell Pike, Paul meets the folk who run one of the country's most scenic railways, has a close encounter with a vulture, and gets to grips with the locals in a spot of Cumberland wrestling

programme.title: A Very British Murder with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: An examination of the dark history behind Britain's fascination with murder, beginning with historian and curator Lucy Worsley exploring how notorious killings were transformed into popular entertainment in the first half of the 19th century

programme.title: Sacred Songs - The Secrets of Our Hearts
programme.desc: British vocal ensemble Tenebrae give a special performance of music for Easter, with all 20 of its singers filmed and recorded separately, within their enforced isolation at home, under the direction of Nigel Short. The repertoire includes Bach: Wenn ich einmal so scheiden, Lobo: Versa est in luctum, Allegri: Miserere, Purcell/Croft: Thou knowest, Lord, the secrets of our hearts, Parry: My soul, there is a country and Bach: Ach Herr, lass dein lieb engelein

programme.title: Sounds of the 60s
programme.desc: The BBC's music archive programme reaches the end of the sixties and features performances by Pink Floyd, Donovan, Fleetwood Mac and Joni Mitchell

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Bruno Brookes hosts the edition first aired on November 15, with performances by artists including Black Box, Inspiral Carpets, Robert Palmer and UB40, Caron Wheeler, Jive Bunny and the Mastermixers, Megabass, Kim Appleby, the Righteous Brothers and Prince

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Anthea Turner hosts the edition first aired on November 22, with performances by artists including EMF, Vanilla Ice, the Proclaimers, Rod Stewart and Tina Turner, Julee Cruise, Jimmy Somerville, 808 State and the Righteous Brothers. Plus, Bruno Brookes, Liz Kershaw and the Radio 1 DJ Posse's rendition of Let's Dance

programme.title: Fleetwood Mac's Songbird: Christine McVie
programme.desc: While she has never fronted Fleetwood Mac, preferring to align herself with 'the boys' in the rhythm section, Christine McVie is their most successful singer-songwriter whose hits include Over My Head, Don't Stop and Everywhere. After global success in the late '70s and mid-'80s Christine left the band in 1998, living in semi-retirement in Kent, only to rejoin in 2014. Here, she finally gets to take centre stage to tell both her own story and that of Fleetwood Mac from her vantage point. Contributors include Christine's current bandmates Stevie Nicks, Mick Fleetwood, John McVie, Neil Finn and Mike Campbell

programme.title: Fleetwood Mac - Don't Stop
programme.desc: Documentary looking back over the band's long career, from the early blues outfit led by Peter Green in the late 1960s, to their reincarnation in the 1970s with the line-up that went on to record the 40-million-selling album Rumours. Mick Fleetwood, John McVie, Lindsey Buckingham and Stevie Nicks reveal the truth behind the successes, failures and relationships that characterised their music and made them one of the most enduring acts in popular music

programme.title: A Musical History
programme.desc: Fleetwood Mac are the great survivors of British and American rock music. For more than 50 years they've overcome break-ups and breakdowns to become one of the most successful bands of all time, selling over 100 million records worldwide, with their 1977 smash Rumours accounting for nearly half of those sales. Here, fan Edith Bowman provides a narrative overview, alongside fellow celebrity admirers who all pay tribute to the band. Contributors include KT Tunstall, Travis' frontman Fran Healy, Toyah Willcox, Sian Pattenden and Emma Dabiri

programme.title: Sounds of the 60s
programme.desc: The BBC's music archive programme reaches the end of the sixties and features performances by Pink Floyd, Donovan, Fleetwood Mac and Joni Mitchell

programme.title: Sacred Songs - The Secrets of Our Hearts
programme.desc: British vocal ensemble Tenebrae give a special performance of music for Easter, with all 20 of its singers filmed and recorded separately, within their enforced isolation at home, under the direction of Nigel Short. programme.title: Fleetwood Mac's Songbird: Christine McVie

programme.title: Fleetwood Mac - Don't Stop
programme.desc: Documentary looking back over the band's long career, from the early blues outfit led by Peter Green in the late 1960s, to their reincarnation in the 1970s with the line-up that went on to record the 40-million-selling album Rumours. Mick Fleetwood, John McVie, Lindsey Buckingham and Stevie Nicks reveal the truth behind the successes, failures and relationships that characterised their music and made them one of the most enduring acts in popular music

programme.title: Patagonia: Earth's Secret Paradise
programme.desc: A glimpse at the rarely seen South American wilderness, home to a range of creatures from terrain as varied as the Andes to Cape Horn. Cameras reveal the secret lives of pumas and hummingbirds, soar with condors over glacial peaks and explore monkey puzzle forests dating back to the time of the dinosaurs. The extreme kayakers who gain thrills and excitement from traversing the area's raging waterfalls are observed, as are the native cowboys, known as gauchos, as they round up wild horses. Narrated by Santiago Cabrera

programme.title: Voyages of Discovery
programme.desc: Paul Rose visits Ecuador to examine the tale of an 18th-century expedition in which a group of French intellectuals set out to determine the shape of the Earth. The mission was beset by problems from the start as disease, disputes and affairs set the explorers on separate paths and, while great discoveries were made, the team was still to face theft, death and despair

programme.title: The Young Montalbano
programme.desc: A Vigata inhabitant, who has made a habit of showing up at other people's funerals, is found brutally murdered in the street, and only the disappearance of a woman linked to his former employer gives Montalbano his first lead. Detective drama, starring Michele Riondino. In Italian

programme.title: Italy's Invisible Cities
programme.desc: Using the latest 3D scanning technology, Alexander Armstrong and Dr Michael Scott uncover the hidden history of three Italian cities, beginning with Naples, from its earliest Greek and Roman origins. The pair explore the impact of the volcano of Mount Vesuvius, and how it both nurtured the region and exacted a terrible price on the local population. Alexander and Michael also delve into a labyrinth of underground spaces constructed by ruling Bourbon kings in the 19th century, that helped to sustain the city

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Documentary about a fraud scandal in Romania's healthcare system that was exposed after survivors of a nightclub fire began dying in suspicious circumstances while recovering in hospital with non-life-threatening injuries. When a new health minister was subsequently appointed, journalists follow his attempts, in the face of monumental obstacles, to reform a system riddled with corruption

programme.title: Planet Defenders
programme.desc: Malaika is a wildlife film-maker and conservationist. Growing up in India inspired her to want to save the wildlife on her doorstep. She has always been fascinated by elephants and her mission is to investigate why these beautiful animals are is in such danger. The Indian elephant is classified as endangered, and Malaika learns that the biggest threats they face are forest fragmentation and living in captivity. She goes undercover to find out more about elephants in the tourist trade in Indian cities and then visits an elephant hospital run by Wildlife SOS, a charity which rehabilitates elephants rescued from captivity. Some of the elephants here were in chains for over 50 years and now have a chance to enjoy life in the wild. Malaika talks about the importance of being aware of wildlife tourism and encourages us to say no to exploitative elephant rides available on holiday.

programme.title: Planet Defenders
programme.desc: Environmentalist Jack has made films since he was very young and has a loyal following online. More recently he has thrown himself into climate change activism and working to increase awareness of the threats to our planet. His mission is to investigate the growing but sometimes controversial movement to rewild Britain. Jack discovers how the first legalised reintroduction of the beaver to England is having dramatic results. It is re-engineering river systems and creating rich habitats for many other species. He learns that despite the benefits of having this giant rodent around, some people think bringing it back is bad news. Jack travels to the Highlands of Scotland to witness the beauty of a 300-acre area of land that has been allowed to regenerate in its natural way for the past 40 years. programme.title: My Very First
programme.desc: Join four-month-old Annie-Rose and Theo as they discover and explore the world around them through movement. Both children start at home playing with their families. Theo tries pushing and rolling a ball back and forth with his brother and sisters, before playing on his activity mat where he can grab and shake his rattle and kick his legs. Meanwhile, Annie-Rose has a go at grabbing colourful balls and reaching for her soft, cuddly bunny. Mum and dad play a game with her with balloons, as she learns to help them move. Later, Theo goes out with mum to a baby yoga class where he stretches his arms and legs to sing-along songs. Annie-Rose attends a swimming class - singing, swishing and splashing in the pool! When both children get back home, Theo’s mum blows big bubbles in the garden to see how many he can pop, whilst Annie-Rose finishes off on a video call with grandma where she can practice her waving.

Jessica cooks cabbage and spring onions in oil and then adds them to grated carrot, beetroot, potatoes and a tomato and stock mixture. All of these ingredients are combined in a casserole dish. Once they are stirred together, the casserole dish can be placed in the oven to cook. Jessica wears a traditional Ukrainian outfit and serves drinks in a beautiful drinks dispenser. Borscht is famous for being brightly coloured, so will her friends be able to guess that her special ingredient is beetroot?

programme.title: My First
programme.desc: This episode follows Liberty as she celebrates the Christian holiday of Easter with her family. Liberty and her family begin by visiting a farm, where they see spring lambs and learn all about Easter. During their time at the farm, they all go on an epic scavenger hunt where they collect as many eggs as possible. As Liberty has so much fun on the scavenger hunt, she suggests that they have their own easter egg hunt once they get home. Once Liberty and her family have found all of the chocolate eggs, they decide to melt them down and make tasty chocolate flake cakes. After lots of Easter fun, they go to church for a huge celebration. Liberty and her family finish the day with a lovely picnic and an egg rolling competition.

programme.title: What's On Your Head?

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Lola's family are from Spain, a country in southern Europe. She is making a traditional Spanish dish called empanadas for her and her friends. Lola begins by rolling out two pieces of pizza dough into big circles. She cooks one in the oven. Lola adds tomato puree to passata to make the filling. She also adds some canned tuna, green olives, and boiled egg. She seasons the mixture with paprika, black pepper and her special ingredient, oregano. Then Lola spreads the filling over the dough, which has been in the oven. She finishes the recipe by adding the last piece of dough on top and then putting it back in the oven to cook. Will Lola's friends find the empanadas tasty? Will they guess her special ingredient? There's a very exciting Spanish surprise waiting for her friends while they enjoy their meal!

programme.title: Maddie, the Plants and You
programme.desc: Join Maddie on an adventure around the world of trees to see how we can help look after them and our planet. Discover how trees grow, how we can tell the age of trees and how the roots, leaves and bark work. Maddie shows how trees are home to lots of other plants, animals, birds and insects who live on and amongst them and have an important role in our ecosystem. We’ll discover a tree’s leaves make energy using sunlight, so they can keep growing. We learn that a tree is alive, it drinks and breathes and grows just like we do! And we’ll find out about deciduous trees that lose their leaves in winter and evergreens that keep their leaves all year round.

programme.title: Andy's Aquatic Adventures
programme.desc: Andy works at Safari World, the biggest and best safari park on the planet, home to animals from all over the globe. He works there with his boss, Mr Hammond and tech wizard Jen, who monitors all the animals from the Aqua Base. There is a new resident arriving at Safari World, an eight-month-old African elephant called Elly. Jen informs Andy that Elly is unfortunately an orphan. There are already some adult African elephants at Safari World, so Andy suggests they will be able to look after her. Mr Hammond calls Jen and Andy but looks a little concerned. He wants to know at what age elephants learn to swim, as Elly is only eight months old and the lake in Safari Park is very deep. Jen checks the park database, which has lots of information about African elephants, but it doesn’t say at what age they learn to swim. However, it does say the best place to see wild African elephants swim is the river Nile. It’s time to go on an aquatic adventure! Donning his adventure gear, Andy takes to th

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Emilio's family are from Cyprus, an island nation in the Mediterranean Sea. Emilio is making two Cypriot recipes for his friends to enjoy. The first is pitta bread! The dough is formed and rolled into ovals and placed on a baking tray. The second is made up of red peppers and his special ingredient, halloumi. They are put onto skewers with an oil, oregano, honey and lemon marinade and put into the oven with his pitta bread. Emilio has made a Cypriot wishing tree for his friends to tie their wishes to. But will they like his recipe when they try it?

programme.title: Topsy and Tim
programme.desc: The twins are excited when grandma gives them brand new bikes with stabilisers. However, Tim soon has a fall

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog

programme.title: Andy's Aquatic Adventures
programme.desc: Andy works at Safari World, the biggest and best safari park on the planet, home to animals from all over the globe. He works there with his boss, Mr Hammond and tech wizard Jen, who monitors all the animals from the Aqua Base. Mr Hammond has just got back from a canoeing trip in Alaska, and Andy is waiting for the mail to arrive. Over the radio, Jen informs Andy that the mail sack is in the Jungle Den. Mr Hammond found a bald eagle feather while in Alaska but had it sent back, so he is also waiting for the mail. Andy finds the mail sack and starts to go through it. Andy makes a terrible discovery as the seaweed he ordered for the aquatic plants collection has leaked all over the mail - including Mr Hammonds bald eagle feather. Everything is ruined! It’s time to go on an aquatic adventure! Donning his adventure gear, Andy takes to the skies in his safari-mobile and jets off to Alaska. Andy almost flies into two bald eagles fighting in mid-air, causing the safari-mobile to crash-land in

programme.title: My Very First
programme.desc: Join seven-month-old Rosie and Fionn as they discover the world around them and see how their eyesight is improving. Fionn and his brother Odhrán are playing a game of peek-a-boo with mum and dad at home. Then the whole family head over to Loch Ness, where there are lots of exciting things to see. They even use some binoculars! Over in Ireland, Rosie’s brother Donnacha picks some colourful flowers at the garden centre to take home and show him. Back at home, Fionn is seeing more than his lunch, but what else can he see? In Rosie’s garden they plant their flowers and are inspired to finger-paint using all the colours from the rainbow, while Fionn’s family does a treasure hunt in the garden. They have to find their presents fast as someone on four legs is also hunting! Rosie’s family finish the day with a great game of hide-and-seek, searching high and low until everyone is found.

programme.title: The Toddler Club at Home

programme.title: Treasure Champs
programme.desc: Barry and Kari learn to work together by building an igloo

programme.title: Colours
programme.desc: Series for young children teaching them about colour. In this episode, we learn about objects associated with the colour green, from grass to grapes. We explore the meaning of being green, and the episode concludes with a specially written poem summarising the colour green.

programme.title: CBeebies Presents
programme.desc: William Shakespeare is back on CBeebies with an adaptation of one of his most famous plays, Romeo and Juliet. With the help of a host of CBeebies' stars and actors, Rebecca the Librarian and William Shakespeare put on a performance of the play in a magical library. The story twists and turns as the Montague and Capulet families squabble, and Romeo and Juliet meet and fall in love. There are songs and dances to join in with as the greatest love story ever told comes to life in CBeebies' latest stage show performance.

programme.desc: How a brave mother fought back against car thieves as they tried to steal her car from her driveway - with her baby inside. Narrated by Rav Wilding

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: Special Operations Paramedics Mark and Leigh are called to a car crash in Livingston, where a patient needs to be fully immobilised after complaining of neck pain. Paramedic Gary and Technician Pete attend a patient in Barrhead who has been coughing up blood and needs urgent assessment. Meanwhile, in Edinburgh, Rapid Response Paramedic John is called to a patient with back pain after a fall who is reluctant to go to hospital

programme.title: BBC Wales Today Coronavirus Update
programme.desc: The latest developments, including the daily Welsh Government news conference

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales
