programme.title: S.W.A.T. (2003)
programme.desc: A cop's career is seemingly over when he accidentally shoots a hostage while thwarting a bank robbery. He is given the chance to redeem himself by joining an elite unit headed by a veteran sergeant. Their mission is to escort an international crime lord in custody as he is taken to prison. However, they hit a snag when their prisoner offers a multimillion-dollar reward to anyone who breaks him out of jail, placing them under attack from every criminal in the city. Action thriller, starring Samuel L Jackson and Colin Farrell

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Chef Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and comedians Rhod Gilbert, Rufus Hound and Miranda Hart are the guests on the comedy panel show. Team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack guide the contestants as they try to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves. Presented by Rob Brydon

programme.title: Room 101
programme.desc: Frank Skinner presents the light-hearted panel show, which sees comedy actress Miranda Hart, TV and radio host Reggie Yates and Countryfile presenter John Craven competing to have their pet hates banished to the infamous room for ever. Their gripes include stuffed marrows, spitting, the hip-hop handshake and Miranda's breasts

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Last week saw the return of the comedy show in which three comedians take the stage of the Hammersmith Apollo in west London. The premise is simple. One comic acts as host, warming up the audience before introducing short sets by two other stand-ups. Future editions promise the likes of Ed Byrne, Rob Beckett, Joel Dommett, Henning Wehn, Lucy Porter, but tonight Nish Kumar plays host, before handing over to David O'Doherty and Luisa Omielan

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Mel Giedroyc takes over the reins for the extended edition of the topical quiz, with comedian Hal Cruttenden and writer Sathnam Sanghera joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the stories making the headlines

programme.title: Drug Wars
programme.desc: Police crack down on the gangs that use local businesses as a front for selling drugs, executing raids on these small-time dealers in the hope of crippling the larger players

programme.title: Drug Wars
programme.desc: In the Philippines, President Rodrigo Duterte declares open season on anyone caught dealing or using drugs. Soon after, the DEA heads out on a massive multi-target drugs raid

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The InterContinental Shanghai Wonderland, a 337-room hotel that burrows 16 stories below ground level, clinging to the cliffs of an abandoned quarry

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: How the Abrams Tank, the US Army's biggest land combat vehicle, is constructed, using access to the factory where they are built, as well as their testing grounds

programme.title: Murder Maps
programme.desc: Examining the 1966 murders of three police officers in Shepherd's Bush that led to a huge manhunt and calls for the death penalty to be reinstated

programme.title: History Hunters
programme.desc: The team acquire a personalised Victorian pistol and try to track down the original owner. A customer brings in brasses that his dad brought back from the Somme

programme.title: History Hunters
programme.desc: A customer brings in a family war letter, describing a man's fear for his life before battle. And animal lover Marie investigates the story of a medal awarded for rescuing a dog

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on train journeys through Edwardian Britain, exploring the grand estates of Norfolk and a bivouac on Brownsea Island on the way. He also learns the ropes aboard an Edwardian wherry on the Norfolk Broads

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores an Edwardian utopia in Letchworth, where he meets a fourth generation citizen who introduces him to the town's community spirit. He then heads towards London, and discovers a favoured haunt of King Edward VII, before sampling the monarch's favourite tipple, the King's Ginger

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo arrives in Croydon, where he hears the story of a mixed-race composer who was once celebrated but is now forgotten. At the Three Bridges station, he is delighted to find a recently built depot and its fleet of new Class 700 trains - and is given the great honour of washing one down. In Lewes, he makes a beeline for Charleston, the home of artists Vanessa Bell and Duncan Grant, and in Shoreham-by-Sea, Michael discovers how magnificent Edwardians in flying machines took off from the oldest licensed airport in the country

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo heads to Chichester, West Sussex, where he encounters an Edwardian motoring duo patronised by King Edward VII. After a grand tour of their factory, he is treated to some rest and relaxation in one of their finest vehicles. Arriving in the resort of Southsea, Hampshire, Michael heads for the King's Theatre, where he treads the boards in a community production of Lads in the Village. Crossing the Solent, the presenter boards the Island Line to travel along the pier in a 1930s London Underground carriage, then hops on to the Isle of Wight heritage line bound for Wootton

programme.title: The Architecture The Railways Built
programme.desc: Tim Dunn rides on a funicular railway powered entirely by water. Also, how an abandoned Victorian railway tunnel in Northamptonshire is being transformed into a racetrack

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: An eerie, empty country village which hides a sad secret, a mysterious tower battered by the elements and a disused railway deep beneath the streets of a city

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A settlement in Argentina destroyed by floods, a town in the US that has been erased, and a Soviet dream-town that turned into a nuclear nightmare

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The award-winning documentary series reveals how the end of the Second World War resulted in Britain's military and economic exhaustion, leaving Europe at the mercy of sparring superpowers as the Cold War era dawned. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Jeremy Isaacs' classic documentary explores the Second World War's powerful legacy. For many people the conflict was the most significant experience of their lives, but it also left millions dead - ordinary civilians and victims of persecution, as well as military casualties. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo arrives in Croydon, where he hears the story of a mixed-race composer who was once celebrated but is now forgotten. At the Three Bridges station, he is delighted to find a recently built depot and its fleet of new Class 700 trains - and is given the great honour of washing one down. In Lewes, he makes a beeline for Charleston, the home of artists Vanessa Bell and Duncan Grant, and in Shoreham-by-Sea, Michael discovers how magnificent Edwardians in flying machines took off from the oldest licensed airport in the country

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo heads to Chichester, West Sussex, where he encounters an Edwardian motoring duo patronised by King Edward VII. After a grand tour of their factory, he is treated to some rest and relaxation in one of their finest vehicles. Arriving in the resort of Southsea, Hampshire, Michael heads for the King's Theatre, where he treads the boards in a community production of Lads in the Village. Crossing the Solent, the presenter boards the Island Line to travel along the pier in a 1930s London Underground carriage, then hops on to the Isle of Wight heritage line bound for Wootton

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: In Dorset, Michael Portillo visits Brownsea Island, where Robert Baden Powell began the scout movement in the early 20th century. Joining a group of present scouts, the presenter learns to build a bivouac and finds out what it is about scouting that still appeals to boys - and girls - today. In the New Forest, Michael searches out some unsung heroes of the First World War - the ponies who became war horses - and hears their story from a commoner who runs a stable and riding school. Then in Bournemouth, the one-time MP investigates a very British institution - the beach hut

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on the first leg of a journey from the coalfields of South Wales to the southernmost tip of Cornwall, travelling from Whitland, Carmarthenshire, to Swansea

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave goes to Wales to bag a Sierra Cosworth, a sought-after car in the bidding market. Dad Derek gets his hands on a more sedate 1926 Standard Tourer - a quirky little motor

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek collects a Jaguar Mark 2 and explains why they made such poor getaway cars for 1960s bank robbers. A customer considers a Morris Marina as a Christmas present for his wife

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: How the morale of the Third Reich, boosted by Germany's initial military successes, was shattered by the devastating defeat at Stalingrad, relentless Allied bombing raids and an attempt on Hitler's life. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Laurence Olivier narrates the story of the D-Day landings in June 1944, when the Allies launched the biggest ever amphibious invasion on the beaches of Normandy. Although both sides suffered heavy casualties, it proved to be the turning point in the war against Germany

programme.title: Living With Hitler
programme.desc: We look at the racially unified People's Community, the diplomatic successes of the Third Reich, growing anti-Semitism across Germany, and the lead up to WWII

programme.title: Buried War
programme.desc: Professor David O'Keefe, film-maker, Wayne Abbott and collector Craig Mitchell explore the site of the Battle of Passchendaele, uncovering rare artefacts from the First World War

programme.title: Britain's Biggest Warship
programme.desc: Aircraft carrier HMS Queen Elizabeth sets out on its first transatlantic crossing, a four-month deployment on the high seas which has the primary purpose of putting the top secret F-35B Lightning Stealth Fighter to the test. Within hours of leaving Portsmouth, the vessel encounters a Russian warship and needs to take immediate evasive action, and the crew has to deal with floods and dangerous fuel leaks on their way to Florida

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: A look at the history of personal flight, culminating in Richard Browning and his team of engineers' development of the Gravity Industries Jet Suit

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: An insight into a team of engineers in Perth's attempts to not only break the 763mph land speed record set back in 1997, but also go past the 1,000mph barrier

programme.title: Life in the Undergrowth
programme.desc: The story of the invertebrates that left the sea to move on to land 400 million years ago. Since then, they have become the most successful group of animals, without which the land's ecosystem would collapse. David Attenborough examines the insects' behaviour, travelling to South Africa and Venezuela to witness the habits and routines of millipedes and centipedes

programme.title: Life in the Undergrowth
programme.desc: David Attenborough tells the tale of winged insects, witnessing mayflies emerging from their larval cases in central Hungary and purple crow butterflies in the valleys of Taiwan. Slow-motion cameras reveal the incredible flight skills that enable the creatures to find new territories and sources of food. The aeronautical tricks employed by heavier insects such as bees and beetles are also revealed

programme.title: Life in the Undergrowth
programme.desc: An exploration of how invertebrates' production of silk 300 million years ago became their greatest invention. Stronger than a steel thread of the same diameter, the material is as pliable as elastic and employed by spiders for activities as diverse as courtship, catching prey and tending their young. David Attenborough reveals how some communal spiders create silken palaces rising 15m to 20m into the forest canopy

programme.title: Life in the Undergrowth
programme.desc: David Attenborough turns his attention to the network of partnerships that invertebrates have developed with one another and with plants. He reveals how the fairy wasp, the world's smallest insect, flies underwater to inject its eggs into those of the water beetle. Footage also shows how the caterpillar of the blue butterfly smuggles its way into an ants' nest by mimicking the smell of their young

programme.title: Life in the Undergrowth
programme.desc: David Attenborough explores how insects compensate for their small size by banding together in vast numbers. He reveals that the operations of many of these societies are so finely tuned they appear to work as a single entity. Footage shows how African termite mounds act as huge ventilation systems of such sophistication that humans are only now beginning to unravel the mechanics of the structure

programme.title: Wild Caribbean
programme.desc: The varied landscape of the tropical islands and the species that inhabit them, from flamingos and four-eyed fish to anteaters and cave-dwelling rodents. The programme also delves deeper to explore the islands' destructive history of volcanic eruptions, dangerous reefs and powerful hurricanes

programme.title: Wild Caribbean
programme.desc: The coral and shipwrecks found off the shores of the tropical islands, and how the different kinds of reef have given rise to a huge diversity of underwater life in the area. Underwater time-lapse photography also shows how carnivorous corals feed at night and how, over a few nights of the year, they disperse their sperm and eggs

programme.title: Wild Caribbean
programme.desc: The effects of the region's powerful hurricanes on the islands' wildlife and the survival stories of some of the species, including how the Puerto Rican parrot almost became extinct. Offshore, lobsters head to deeper waters for refuge, while reefs benefit from the destruction

programme.title: Wild Caribbean
programme.desc: The region's mysterious side comes to light during a journey from the rainforests of Panama to the Barrier Reef of the Americas - the world's second largest coral system. The programme also explores the wildlife on the shores of Central America, including killer whales and thimble jellyfish in the deep waters near Honduras

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo ventures onto the European rail network to retrace journeys featured in Bradshaw's Continental Railway Guide of 1913, beginning with London to Monte Carlo. His first stop is Paris, where he absorbs the atmosphere, before heading south to the Cote d'Azur and ending his journey at Monaco's gaming tables

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Armed with his trusty copy of Bradshaw's Continental Railway Guide of 1913, Michael Portillo explores the former domain of the Habsburg monarchs, beginning in the Hungarian capital Budapest. He then travels to Austria via Bratislava in Slovakia, and in Vienna he immerses himself in pre-First World War decadence. From there he heads to Salzburg, before going on to the Salzkammergut region to visit the emperor's Austrian summer residence at Bad Ischl, where in 1914 Franz Joseph I signed a declaration that changed the course of European history

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels through Germany, beginning his journey in Berlin - which at the beginning of the 20th Century was a powerhouse of science and technology. Led by his copy of Bradshaw's Continental Railway Guide of 1913, he heads west via the Harz Mountains to the industrial Ruhr Valley to learn how imperial Germany was ready for war. He then goes south along the tourist trail of the castle-studded Rhine river and ends in the Rheingau to taste the wines of its vineyards

programme.title: Warbird Workshop
programme.desc: A group of amateur pilots find their light plane has a glorious war record - and launch an ambitious project to rebuild the Piper Cub and restore its military markings

programme.title: Secrets of the Transport Museum
programme.desc: A historic Frazer Nash car must be fixed before a big race. Plus, the first car to go round the track is resurrected but will the museum director be able to drive it?

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave goes to Wales to bag a Sierra Cosworth, a sought-after car in the bidding market. Dad Derek gets his hands on a more sedate 1926 Standard Tourer - a quirky little motor

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: The team is under pressure with a record number of cars to auction, but Derek finds room for one more - a replica AC Cobra. A couple are reunited with their Volvo breakdown truck

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A classic car fan with a love of the Jaguar E-Type reluctantly sends his dream car to auction, hoping to buy a motorhome with the money. Two Ford Escorts get Dave's pulse racing

programme.title: Britain's Biggest Warship
programme.desc: The captain of HMS Queen Elizabeth deliberately seeks out the worst possible weather so he can test how the F35 jets cope under extreme conditions - and gets his wish when the aircraft carrier ends up in the path of a hurricane. After consulting with his officers, he resolves to brave the elements and test the jets to the brink of destruction. The crew also faces the challenge of refueling the vessel at sea - one of the hardest tasks outside of going into battle

programme.title: Britain's Biggest Warship
programme.desc: The crew of HMS Queen Elizabeth completes the vessel's sea trials, testing an innovative new firefighting system for the first time and marking the 100th anniversary of a plane being successfully landed on a moving ship. The carrier returns to Portsmouth, which will be its home port for the next 50 years, while the pilots of its fighter jets go to the US for additional training

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: A look onboard the $200million Black Pearl, which is the largest sailing yacht in the world and has been built to sail faster than any ship of its size

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: A look at the Qinghai-Tibet line, the highest railway in the world, providing an insight into the engineering challenges faced at high altitude and in bitterly cold temperatures

programme.title: Wild Japan
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the wildlife of rural Japan's mountains, rainforests, beaches and swamps, beginning with Honshu, Japan's largest island

programme.title: Wild Japan
programme.desc: The tropical Southwest Islands in the south of Japan, which are home to volcanoes and fierce typhoons

programme.title: Wild Japan
programme.desc: The wildlife of rural Japan's mountains, rainforests and beaches, where the climate can shift drastically from icy cold winters to tropical summers

programme.title: Galapagos
programme.desc: High-definition cameras and aerial photography techniques are used to explore the archipelago, which is rich in wildlife and made famous by Charles Darwin's On the Origin of the Species. This programme focuses on the geological history of the islands through the evidence of volcanic eruptions and examines what makes it unique. Tilda Swinton narrates

programme.title: Galapagos
programme.desc: Examining the lesser-known parts of the islands and the species that have successfully evolved to suit the environment. The programme discovers how tortoise shells have changed shape, flowers have turned yellow to attract bees, and finches have become warblers. Plus, how flightless cormorants are still hunting underwater. Narrated by Tilda Swinton

programme.title: Galapagos
programme.desc: An insight into how creatures adapt to the islands' extremes of weather, including iguanas that climb more than 1,000 metres to lay eggs, fish forced to rely on the shade provided by mangroves, and seals that survive on exposed shorelines by taking shelter in lava grottos

programme.title: Thailand: Earth's Tropical Paradise
programme.desc: Exploring the wildlife and culture of the Southeast Asian nation. The first episode examines the south of the country, a popular tourist destination for Westerners, and examines the role of spirituality in daily life. Narrated by Sophie Okonedo

programme.title: Thailand: Earth's Tropical Paradise
programme.desc: In central Thailand's forests, fertile plains and even its city streets, nature finds a way of living alongside people, and spirituality can be found in human and animal relationships, both likely and unlikely. This bustling region is known as the nation's rice bowl - but even here, there are magical places to be found. Narrated by Sophie Okonedo

programme.title: Thailand: Earth's Tropical Paradise
programme.desc: The mountains and forests of Northern Thailand, an area that is home to a variety of wildlife, leading to unusual partnerships developing between animals and people. Narrated by Sophie Okonedo

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Following in the footsteps of early 20th-century travellers, Michael Portillo uses his 1913 copy of Bradshaw's Continental Railway Guide to explore Switzerland, which was a favourite with Edwardian tourists. He begins in Basel, travels east to visit the industrial regions of Zurich and learns about the engineering feats needed to thread the rail network through the Alps. He takes in the striking beauty of Lake Lucerne, before ending with an ascent by train to Europe's highest station at Jungfraujoch

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo uses his 1913 copy of Bradshaw's Continental Railway Guide to explore Holland and Belgium, before travelling to the French sector of the Western Front. He ends his journey in the Forest of Compiegne in northern France to hear how the First World War armistice was signed in a railway carriage

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo once again ventures onto the European rail network to retrace journeys featured in George Bradshaw's 1913 publication Continental Guide. He begins in Spain, where he visits the scene of an assassination attempt at a royal wedding in Madrid, before dancing with an unusual partner in Cordoba. He investigates the story behind Bizet's Carmen at a tobacco factory in Seville, learns about Winston Churchill's tense diplomatic mission to Algeciras and finishes with tales of British espionage in Gibraltar

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Fletcher helps cellmate Godber make it through the night, calling on his unpredictable and limitless imagination to while away the hours. Classic comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and Richard Beckinsale

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: The inmates face a day of hard labour digging drains on a lonely stretch of moorland. However, Fletcher soon finds a way of turning the situation to his advantage. Prison comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and Richard Beckinsale

programme.title: Porridge

programme.title: Guitar Heroes at the BBC
programme.desc: Archive footage of guitarists taken from BBC music shows of the 1970s. Includes electric performances by Carlos Santana, Mark Knopfler, Peter Green and the Edge, and acoustic material by John Martyn, John Renbourn, Bert Jansch and Paco Pena

programme.title: Guitar Heroes at the BBC
programme.desc: A celebration of 1970s-era axe-men, acoustic virtuosos and thumping riff merchants, in a compilation of guitar-heavy performances from the BBC TV archives. programme.title: Guitar Heroes at the BBC

programme.desc: The chef travels to La Grange, a suburb of Chicago, to help the owners of Cafe 36, an upmarket bistro threatening to destroy the marriage of its owners due to the scale of their financial losses. The head chef's attitude to quality control is driving customers away, and it is up to Gordon Ramsay to turn the business around

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin meets holistic therapist Daniela from East Yorkshire, who is looking to find a property on the coastline of western Madeira to live with her two rescue dogs. She wants a property with sea views, lots of outside space and the potential for a therapy room. With Daniela's budget of £100,000 and her mother Janet by her side to help, Jonnie Irwin lines up five options for her to choose between

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Joanne and Dean from Hertfordshire have been together for six years and have been enjoying holidays in Tenerife ever since. They love it so much that they've decided to take the next step and buy their very own holiday home in Costa Adeje, so call upon Ben Hillman to find five properties for them to choose between, within their budget of £150,000

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer look back at two shows featuring families who needed to reconfigure their ground-floor layouts. Graham and Nardeth bought their detached Windsor home 10 years ago, but while he loves Windsor and sees it as their dream home, she is tired of the cramped interior. Enter Kirstie, knocking down walls in the hope of proving the house was worth keeping. Plus, Northamptonshire couple Louis and Isobel are desperate to enjoy their retirement - but it will take a £70,000 refurbishment to reconfigure the ground floor. Even then, will they decide to stay or sell up?

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick and Angel Strawbridge turn their attentions to the wash house - a shell of an old stone building on the far side of the moat. They have visions of turning it into a bathroom for their 40-foot boat on the moat, complete with all eco-friendly mod cons. And it's not long before the whole family get to work clearing the overgrown site so the project can begin

programme.title: The Great Celebrity Bake Off for Stand Up to Cancer
programme.desc: Matt Lucas hosts as TV presenter Stacey Dooley, Little Mix's Jade Thirlwall, comedian Katherine Ryan and internet influencer KSI show off their baking skills. They serve up cocktail-flavoured fondant fancies, have their knowledge of pastry tested in the technical and in the showstopper are asked to create a scene showing something they cannot live without - all from biscuit and meringue. Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith judge their efforts

programme.title: First Dates Hotel
programme.desc: Six-foot gym owner and ex-military man Richard is paired with Emilia. Despite his muscular appearance, he is a sensitive soul who loves musicals and Harry Potter, and his Asperger's leaves him unable to read what others are thinking, so mid-date he is forced to ask Emilia how he is doing. Meanwhile, 40-year-old hotelier Andy arrives at Vesuvius for his first ever date with a man, 37-year-old bouncer Joseph, and police officer Angie hooks up with car salesman Tom, who is determined to leave his stereotypical lad image behind

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Contestants Charlotte Ritchie, Jamali Maddix, Lee Mack, Mike Wozniak and Sarah Kendall vie once more, as Taskmaster Greg Davies presides. On this edition, Lee attempts an audacious hammer throw, Sarah makes sitting down memorable and Jamali forgets his passport. As usual, Alex Horne offers the intrepid contenders moral support

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: This edition follows the stories of people who have lived through tragedy and life-changing circumstances, exploring how they've overcome their loss and adapted to the new paths their lives have taken. Steve, 52, is rushed to St George's after falling over and hitting his head on a concrete step, while 21-year-old Fleur is assessed after she woke up with pain and swelling in her leg. Muffy, 72, is brought to Resus with slurred speech and weakness in her legs, and doctors suspect she may have suffered a stroke

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets Spanish architect Jaime and his wife Mimi as they embark on a mission to convert a Grade II listed folly into a family home in Buckinghamshire. Giving themselves a wildly optimistic six-month deadline to finish before the birth of their second child, it's not long before the couple hit trouble. Jaime puts all his energies and creativity into the project, using innovative 3D mapping to squeeze living spaces into the tower, but it proves to be an exhausting struggle

programme.title: George Clarke's Old House, New Home
programme.desc: George's continued renovation mission takes him to Moseley and Wheaton Aston in the West Midlands. He begins with a conversion to unite upstairs and downstairs flats in an Arts and Crafts building, although one of his suggestions leaves the owners speechless. He also visits an 18th-century pub that has fallen victim to some dodgy DIY - and quickly devises a clever plan to make the most of the period features

programme.title: Strangers Making Babies
programme.desc: Documentary following single women looking for platonic partners to have children with. Businesswoman Trinity from Essex originally wanted to raise a child on her own, until a health scare made her realise how important it is for a child to have two parents. She is matched with three potential partners. Nanny Venicia and amateur ballroom dancer Sarah also spend more time with the men they have been matched with. The time eventually comes to decide which potential dads-to-be they want to take to the next stage. But could romantic advances get in the way of a co-parenting partnership?

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Mother-and-daughter team Annelies and Claudia Richardson host the week's last visit at the Fauconberg Arms in Coxwold, North Yorkshire. Their ethos is that food is just as important as the rooms, but this threatens to prove their downfall when Rick and Sarah have reservations about cleanliness

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: A British couple in Spain want to bring in more money by opening a restaurant, but struggle to get a licence. Meanwhile, an expat in France tries to run a B&B on his own

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Kelly from Essex would love a holiday home in the Black Sea coastal resort of Sunny Beach in Bulgaria. She's looking for an apartment on a complex, with plenty of facilities and a pool. With a budget of £25,000 and Kelly's best friend Angie coming along to help, will any of Scarlette Douglas's five fantastic properties turn out to be the perfect package?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Presenter Sara Damergi helps Chris Grandison and his grandmother Patricia Blythe search for a holiday home in Malta. Patricia and her late husband lived on the island many years ago when he served in the Armed Forces, and her grandson hopes to find a property with rental potential on a budget of £70,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Ian Tan and his partner Christine Walsh hope to buy a pad near Sarasota in Florida. With a flexible budget of £150,000 to £250,000, the pair peruse a selection of options along the Gulf Coast. Presented by Jonnie Irwin

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Fitness instructor Liza Macias hosts the first party in Warwickshire and hopes wooing her diners with a French menu and three hunky waiters will be enough to win her the £1,000 prize at the end of the week

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Sailor Steve Aston hosts the second dinner party in Warwickshire, hoping his nautical-themed evening will impress the women in the group and win him the £1,000 prize. But what will the ladies think when they discover the secrets of his 'love ship'?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Admin manager Emma Reddin hosts the third dinner party in Warwickshire, and hopes to secure the £1,000 prize by offering her guests pea and potato samosas and a selection of Indian curries, and organising Bollywood dancing

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: PA Pinky Sidhu hosts the fourth dinner party in Warwickshire, hoping to educate her guests about the finer things in life with an evening of sophistication and pageantry. However, the diners quickly find fault with her starter and dessert, and there is a surprise winner of the beauty competition she arranged as entertainment

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Driving instructor Alison Firth hosts the final dinner party in Warwickshire, and hopes that her 1980s disco-themed night featuring a marshmallow competition will impress her guests enough to see her win the cash prize

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Fiery chef Gordon Ramsay comes to the aid of a mother-and-son team who have gone through 20 chefs in two years at their struggling Italian restaurant in California. The adjoining bar is a triumph, but the oldest dining establishment in town needs an overhaul if the inexperienced owners are to make a profit

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Recently retired Baksho wants to buy a holiday home in the resort of Gran Alacant on Spain's popular Costa Blanca. With a budget of £100,000 and her sister Sukvinder by her side to help, Jasmine Harman sets out to find Baksho the perfect family friendly property

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Danni Menzies helps a couple who run a pub-restaurant in County Durham to find a home on the Greek island of Kefalonia on a budget of £120,000

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: The architect meets a West Sussex couple planning to build a house on the back of an old trailer, while in Wiltshire, a single mother plans to transform an old military helicopter, and a water tower undergoes a surprising conversion in Warwickshire. George's tour of Spain continues in northern Andalusia, where he visits a cave-style retreat quarried deep into the mountains

programme.title: 24 Hours in Police Custody
programme.desc: The police investigate an apparently unprovoked attack that has left a man fighting for his life after being shot in the chest at point blank range by a complete stranger. programme.title: The Dog House
programme.desc: An 11-year-old is thrilled to meet his dream dog - Gizmo the pug. However, Wood Green staff are worried his excitement might send breathless Gizmo into a panic. programme.title: Jamie: Keep Cooking Family Favourites
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver demonstrates a simple recipe for a one-pan wonder chicken and pepper curry, served with a flatbread flavoured with a hit of mustard. programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second of this week's destinations is The Falcon Retreat in South Wales. The view makes a big impression, but things go less well when the guests inspect their rooms

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week takes place at The Crown Inn in Grewelthorpe, North Yorkshire, where owners Debbie Heartfield and Paul Dixon hope to impress their guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final venue is Stix Glamping in Greetham, Rutland, where one guest decides glamping is not for her before she has even set foot on site, leaving her partner all alone

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart plays a prank on Mrs Krabappel, but it backfires badly and results in her being fired, leaving him feeling so guilty that he resolves to do everything he can to get her reinstated - even if it means discrediting her replacement

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: During a trip to a water park with the family, Grampa bumps into a newspaper columnist who seemingly becomes fascinated with his life and writes a series of stories about him, but Homer becomes suspicious of the journalist's motives

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Dracula, the Wolf Man, the Mummy and Frankenstein's monster gatecrash a Halloween party at 742 Evergreen Terrace. Bart and Lisa plot revenge on their teachers in a Hitchcock-inspired short story, but end up turning on each another

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge poses for a charity calendar and becomes Springfield's number-one sex symbol, while Carl gets a promotion at the plant, before going on a power trip and hiring Homer as his new assistant

programme.title: The Simpsons

programme.title: Filthy House SOS
programme.desc: Extreme cleaners Richard and Brennan travel to Gateshead to help 40-year-old sci-fi collector Abi, whose massive horde of toys, posters and films has completely taken over her home

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Two ministers in Liverpool take over an old church with a plan to turn it into a community centre, only to be obstructed by a group of squatters who refuse to leave. Time to call in eviction expert Chris Sharpe. Council officers Shital and Andrew are on the warpath when a neglectful landlord ignores their warnings about fire safety. Northamptonshire landlord Sina visits her property to ask why payments had stopped, only to be faced by an angry and volatile tenant, leaving her scared to return

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: The documentary about landlord and tenancy issues catches up on some of the stories featured so far. Emmanuel reveals what happened after he returned from a year working abroad to find himself £24,000 in mortgage arrears and unable to get into his own property. Andy made a mistake when he rented out his north London home to someone he knew, only for his so-called friend to sublet the property, pocket the profits and pay Andy nothing. What has happened since?

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: The officers try to recover £13,000 owed by a high-performance car dealer in Birmingham, and in Wolverhampton the agents chase nearly £2,000 owed to a car hire company. Elsewhere, officers visit a family in Kent to try to collect more than £5,000, but an emotional backstory means they are forced to make a sensitive decision

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Charlie and Wayne are in hot pursuit of a van, but the driver crashes the vehicle and runs off into the night. With the help of a tracker, officers hunt down a 17-year-old suspected of committing a serious robbery, while Matt and Brimmers are on the hunt for the driver of a £300,000 Rolls-Royce who is thought to be under the influence

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Pursued by a helicopter and a team of armed response vehicles, two men armed with machetes and a sawn-off shotgun make a dramatic escape on a motorbike. Meanwhile, officers Clarkie and Nick take two dangerous boy racers off the road, and a high-speed pursuit of alleged burglars turns into a race on foot

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A white van fails to stop and leads officers on night-time pursuit round Halifax, but the chase finally comes to an end with devastating consequences. Sophie finds a drunk driver and his girlfriend on a motorway embankment, having abandoned their car, while Clarkie is forced to use pepper spray after a motorist disappears into some bushes

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: In Staffordshire, the agents chase up a £3,000 deposit owed by a landlord to his tenant, and officers in Birmingham try to recover £25,000 from a nightclub owner for unpaid solicitor fees. Meanwhile, agents Max and Paul are in Surrey as they attempt to collect more than £3,000 owed to a garage after a customer disputed the repairs made to his van

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Officers chase £4,500 in court fees in north Wales after a property deal turned sour, and in Birmingham the agents try to collect over £3,500 from a single mum who defaulted on loan repayments. programme.title: Neighbours

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Another glimpse into the lives of expats who have left the UK for a new life in the Spanish sun. Former Glaswegian taxi drivers Pete and Kath came to Benidorm to retire, only to end up taking over a bar, while fitness instructor and entrepreneur Anthony has organised a campsite coach trip to the Algar waterfalls. Female impersonator James and his partner David have rented a working farm, and it proves a steep learning curve for the pair who have sunk every penny into the project

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: Dr Julian Humphrey and Sister Benita Wainwright treat Phil, who has the rare condition aortic dissection, while Dr Humphrey also tries to put a man's dislocated shoulder into place. Consultant Dr Bharath Reddy and junior Dr George Kay are on standby to treat 14-month-old Heaven, who is rushed in with breathing problems, while volunteer Jane keeps the team and patients fed and watered

programme.title: 999: Critical Condition
programme.desc: A man is rushed in with multiple stab wounds to his abdomen and after the team check whether his organs have been affected, he is rushed in for emergency surgery. Meanwhile, a teenage footballer arrives by helicopter, having lost all sensation below the waist after an accident during a game, and a dairy farmer is wheeled into A&E, having being knocked unconscious by a cow's hoof. Worried about a bleed on the brain, staff fast-track him to CT, aware that any trauma to the brain could affect his ability to do his very physical job

programme.title: A&E After Dark
programme.desc: The hospital security team is called to reception after a suspected drug user is found passed out on the floor, while ex-factory worker Claude is determined to discharge himself after a bang on the head. Sixty-eight-year-old former fire fighter Stephen is brought in after passing out at home, and blood tests reveal that he has sepsis, an infection that causes more deaths than cancer

programme.title: Ambulance: Code Red

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: The officers try to recover £5,500 owed by a travel agent to a dissatisfied customer in east London, and in Birmingham the agents chase over £2,000 owed by a convenience store owner to a supplier. In Staffordshire, two officers try to collect several thousand pounds owed by a pensioner after a dispute with his neighbour went all the way to the High Court

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Agents Max and Steve try to recover almost £2,000 owed by the owner of a bridal shop in London, while officers Gary and Cona in Kent chase over £4,000 of vet fees after two neighbouring dogs got into a fight. In south west London, agents try to collect almost £11,500 owed by a small business

programme.title: Filthy House SOS
programme.desc: Richard and Brennan come to the aid of a grandmother in Oswestry on the Welsh border, whose home is so covered in dog fur and dust that it is hard to breathe inside

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Despite her best efforts, Susan cannot shake the visions of Finn, and admits she might be having a mental health crisis. Fay gets a check-up, and receives devastating news

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Ryder, Chloe, Nikau and Bella find themselves trapped inside a shipping container. Mia hesitantly tries to fix things with Ari, and Tori talks Justin out of buying a house

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Cameras focus on Jan and her son Glen. She moved to Saydo in Andalucia 17 years ago with her husband Ken, who passed away over the summer. To celebrate Ken's love of a good knees-up, Glen's putting on the biggest party Saydo's ever seen. Plus, a look at Benalmadena, where Laura and Phil, together with six of their friends back in the UK, have bought two bars. John Thomson narrates

programme.title: The Bodyguard (1992)
programme.desc: Rachel Marron, a famous singer and actress is subjected to death threats by an obsessive fan and persuaded to hire a former secret service agent as her bodyguard. However, neither she or her new minder realise the effect they are about to have on each other's lives. Romantic thriller directed by Mick Jackson, starring Kevin Costner, Whitney Houston, Gary Kemp and Bill Cobbs

programme.title: The Nightmare Neighbour Next Door
programme.desc: Another selection of residential feuds, featuring Barnsley pensioners Barry and Phyllis Roddis's dispute with a neighbour who was scrapping cars in his driveway, which led to him waging a nine-year campaign of intimidation against them. Plus, in Streatham, south London, Richard and Philomena Chadwick have been battling for five years to evict squatters whose bongo-playing parties and rooftop sunbathing have invaded the privacy of their once secluded garden

programme.title: The Nightmare Neighbour Next Door
programme.desc: The De Ropers' hopes of a peaceful life in Yeovil, Somerset, were rudely shattered by their neighbour Peter Stoodley's 120 noisy chickens, and they spent their first two years in their new home battling to persuade the authorities to ban him from keeping animals. In a quiet Anglesey village, 67-year-old Pamela Booth's discovered that her neighbour had been fraudulently using her bank card to steal more than £1,000 over 10 months, while a 24-year-old wannabe rapper in Dagenham, east London, generated more than 80 complaints after repeatedly keeping residents awake by playing loud music into the early hours in his flat

programme.title: Chocolat (2000)
programme.desc: A woman and her daughter move to a sleepy French village in 1959 and brazenly open a chocolate shop directly opposite the Catholic parish church during Lent. Drama adapted from Joanne Harris's novel, with Juliette Binoche, Johnny Depp, Alfred Molina and Judi Dench

programme.desc: Homicide police hand the case of a retired marine colonel found murdered with an axe over to NCIS when they are unable to solve the case, despite all the circumstantial evidence pointing to the deceased's drug-addicted son. They discover the suspect's mother went missing two years previously and Ziva suggests he may have killed more than one parent, while the family's neighbour is revealed to also have had a motive for wanting the man dead. Crime drama, starring Michael Weatherly and Cote de Pablo

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the murder of a marine working for the Defence Intelligence Agency who was blamed for the disappearance of 500 specialised rifles in Afghanistan. A cryptic message found at the scene, written in the victim's blood, leads the agents on the trail of a controversial manuscript that claims to expose the truth about the missing weapons, and an FBI agent with a secret. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: The officers discover a girl's body in a hospital emergency room, and the subsequent investigation leads to the revelation that she was killed during an unauthorised exorcism carried out by an overzealous nun who claims her behaviour was directed by the voice of an angel. Jerry Orbach and Sam Waterston star

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: The investigation into a fatal attack on a semi-retired divorce lawyer by a disgruntled client reveals a network of bribery and corrupt medical practices. As the team unravels the tangled threads of the case, it appears a trio of doctors may be destined for prosecution - and it is not long before a further murder occurs. Police drama, starring Sam Waterston and Jerry Orbach

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Curtis helps a woman who claims her baby was kidnapped while she was at confession to retrace her steps. However, legal questions begin to appear when her attorney introduces a unique defence. Award-winning police drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Benjamin Bratt

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: When a man goes on a shooting spree in Central Park, Briscoe and new partner Green pull out all the stops to trace the weapon. McCoy, meanwhile, sets his sights on finding the killer - and the person who sold him the gun. Police drama, starring Jerry Orbach, Jesse E Martin and Sam Waterston

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Green investigate the shooting of a civil court judge. Catching the man they think responsible, they bring him to trial - but the victim refuses to implicate him. Award-winning police drama, starring Jerry Orbach

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: The team investigates the murder of a social worker, exposing a racket involving foster children. The resulting trial sees Paul Robinette place the system in the dock as he defends a black woman accused of kidnapping her baby from his adoptive parents. Award-winning police drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Benjamin Bratt

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The agents examine the death of a seaman and find themselves working alongside Barrett. Gibbs makes his displeasure about the circumstances of the case well known, prompting the rest of the investigators to worry that he might be about to leave the NCIS again. Meanwhile, Ziva introduces her new boyfriend to her team-mates. Crime drama, guest starring Enrique Murciano

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A Navy lieutenant is found dead in the Pentagon's custodial closet, and surveillance footage indicates she was killed by a co-worker. However, Ziva and the team discover her supposed assailant actually died of a heart attack days before the crime was committed. Meanwhile, suspicions are raised regarding the eyeball left in Tony's drink, and the investigators' attention is fixed on the port-to-port murderer, who appears to still be at large

programme.title: NCIS

However, a key difference in the way the victim was bound suggests the crime was actually the work of a copycat, forcing the investigators to consider whether Price had any enemies

Detective thriller, starring Tom Selleck and Kathy Baker

Jerry Orbach and Steven Hill star

programme.desc: A hidden anti-Semitic message in a high-school yearbook offers an insight into the murder of an art teacher, and the subsequent case leads McCoy into conflict with 'Klan lawyer' Roy Payne

programme.desc: The detectives are presented with a host of suspects while investigating the murder of a teenage drug user - and begin to suspect a member of her wealthy family could be responsible. Drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Steven Hill

programme.desc: The conviction of a death row prisoner is called into question by the revelation that attorney-client privilege may have prompted a former prosecutor to withhold evidence capable of establishing his innocence. Drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Steven Hill

A lead from Barrett's squad reveals the CIA has also been tracking the murderer - and its findings suggest the culprit could be an even deadlier foe than anticipated

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and the team attend the funeral of a fallen colleague before resuming their hunt for the man they believe to be the port-to-port killer. programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A woman is found murdered and Gibbs and McGee are called in when she is discovered to be carrying ID bearing the name Sgt Patricia Moreno, a US marine known to have died several years earlier. The victim is discovered to have come to America from Afghanistan and a necklace reveals a fingerprint that leads the team to a known assassin. Guest starring Lolita Davidovich

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team's holiday plans are put on hold when a murder investigation leads to the discovery of a newborn baby who has no identification and no apparent ties to a family. Mark Harmon and Sean Murray star

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: The detectives investigate the discovery of a woman's body in the Bronx and learn she was reported missing 20 years previously. programme.desc: Jessica helps a teenage tennis player whose desire to win is being ruined by her domineering father and a tragedy in her past, leading to some complex emotional problems. Angela Lansbury stars

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Recently retired couple Nick and Jane Purser are looking forward to settling down, but cannot decide where that should be. Jane's mother lives in Liverpool, so she would prefer to stay close by with a move to Lancaster. Nick wants them to move to southern France, where he can ride his motorbike in the sun. Jasmine Harman shows the pair three properties in Lancaster for up to £180,000 and Jonnie Irwin tempts them with three properties for up to £145,000 in France

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: When her partner of 20 years died, Ginny Lunn decided to find a holiday home where she could create new memories. Torn between buying a place in Dorset within easy reach of her London home for a budget of £250,000, and the Costa Tropical in Spain, where she would have a budget of £150,000, she requires help. Jasmine Harman shows her three options in Dorset, and Laura Hamilton tries to impress her with three properties in Spain, but, with the help of her sister Sara, which one will Ginny choose?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away

Stuart wants to move to central Scotland, close to family and work. Horse-lover Laura thinks they'll get more property and land in Murcia in Spain. programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away

programme.title: Nothing to Declare
programme.desc: Officers question a man suspected of carrying drugs in his luggage, and a man with poor personal hygiene is sent back home to Prague

programme.title: Nothing to Declare
programme.desc: Immigration officers question a South African man who appears to have a false passport, and a couple discover they have unwittingly imported a deadly disease

programme.title: Nothing to Declare
programme.desc: Customs officers arrest a man carrying heroin internally, and the immigration department is tipped off about a woman working illegally in a restaurant

programme.title: Nothing to Declare
programme.desc: Immigration officials are worried about the mental health of a Korean girl who arrives at Sydney Airport with very little money and no idea of how she will support herself

programme.title: Nothing to Declare
programme.desc: A Chinese national is quizzed about his place of birth. Meanwhile, officers become suspicious regarding a Ferrari and its possible contents

programme.title: Nothing to Declare
programme.desc: Customs officials continue to quiz a Chinese man about his passport, and officers are alerted by Canadian customs that a suspicious passenger is on his way to Australia

programme.title: Monkey Business
programme.desc: Documentary strand on a Dorset chimpanzee sanctuary. Chimp Charlie - which has been affected by a cocktail of drugs - throws a fit, causing chaos among the other apes

programme.title: Monkey Business
programme.desc: Charlie the reformed junkie chimp has his eyesight tested at the Dorset wildlife sanctuary, while a miserable capuchin monkey arrives unexpectedly, and keeper Mike is assigned to look after a pair of love-struck orang-utans

programme.title: Monkey Business
programme.desc: The orang-utan love affair develops as two chimpanzee clans are prepared for their first meeting, and a lemur keeper hand-rears some rejected babies

programme.title: Monkey Business
programme.desc: There is trouble in store when the two chimpanzee clans are introduced. The respective leaders engage in a face off in the outside enclosure

programme.title: Wild Vets
programme.desc: The daily challenges faced by New Zealand's vets as they treat an exotic array of wildlife in the country's zoos

programme.title: Wild Vets
programme.desc: A vet comes face to face with a grumpy lion and another struggles when trying to microchip ducks, while a turtle is released back into the wild

programme.title: Wild Vets
programme.desc: Brett treats an albatross with a sore leg at the wildlife hospital, and at Wellington Zoo a vet tries to help a sun bear suffering from a sore tooth

programme.title: Wild Vets
programme.desc: A team of vets battles to save Sweetpea the penguin's damaged eye, and at Wellington Zoo a pack of African wild dogs faces a round of injections

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Warehouse 13
programme.desc: Part one of two. A pair of secret service agents are transferred from the president's protection team to the secret unit where the collection of every supernatural and unexplained item that the US government has ever claimed is stored. Pilot episode of the sci-fi drama, starring Eddie McClintock, Joanne Kelly and Saul Rubinek

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A special agent disappears during an undercover assignment, forcing the team to work alongside the LAPD in a case that involves human-trafficking, money-laundering and drugs. Starring Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J

programme.title: Magnum P.I.
programme.desc: Reboot of the 1980s crime drama, starring Jay Hernandez as an ex-Navy Seal returning from Afghanistan who decides to ply his trade as a private investigator in Hawaii, while also acting as a security consultant for an estate. With Perdita Weeks

programme.title: Hawaii Five-0
programme.desc: Pilot episode of the American crime drama remake, starring Alex O'Loughlin and Scott Caan. When former Navy Seal Steve McGarrett returns to his native Hawaii to investigate his father's murder, the island's governor enlists him to lead an elite branch of the state's police force, pairing him with New Jersey detective Danny 'Danno' Williams

programme.title: MacGyver
programme.desc: The team goes undercover on a college campus to smoke out a deep-cover operative who is recruiting students to carry out deadly attacks around the world

programme.title: MacGyver
programme.desc: The team drives a tanker filled with liquid oxygen through rebel-occupied territory to a children's hospital. Riley helps Angus decide whether to allow his dad back into his life

programme.title: Merlin
programme.desc: After Arthur kills a unicorn while hunting, Camelot's crops are destroyed and its water supply runs dry. The prince refuses to believe he could be responsible for any curse, but is warned by the animal's keeper that he must make amends or the kingdom will face ruin. Frank Finlay guest stars

programme.title: Merlin
programme.desc: Gwen's father Tom is put on trial by Uther, accused of practising magic by helping a sorcerer turn lead into gold. However, Morgana fears his fate has already been decided, and tries to help him escape before he is sentenced to death. Cal Macaninch guest stars

programme.title: CSI: Crime Scene Investigation
programme.desc: Grissom is required to give evidence in a trial, putting his handling of an old homicide case in the spotlight. It transpires that an incriminating fingerprint found at the scene did not actually belong to the accused, and the team's competence is called into question. Meanwhile, Sara and Greg try to trace a murder weapon back to its owner to solve a mysterious killing. Starring William Petersen

programme.title: Banged Up Abroad
programme.desc: The story of two British backpackers who were ambushed and held to ransom by a guerrilla group while crossing the Darien Gap in Panama

programme.title: Banged Up Abroad
programme.desc: In a bid to improve his family's fortunes, a man decides to smuggle six kilos of cocaine from Venezuela to Ireland, but when he is caught the consequences are dire

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: Halvar tries to make a seagull land on his head as a sign of his rightful leadership - but it keeps landing on Gorm instead

programme.desc: Halvar discovers a cave where Sven has hidden all his loot - and finds that all the words spoken there have become frozen in the ice

programme.title: Dork Hunters from Outer Space
programme.desc: A group of heroes try to stop aliens taking over the universe

programme.title: The Knowledge: The World's Toughest Taxi Test
programme.desc: A behind-the-scenes look at the test that all black cab drivers working in the city of London must take, one of the world's toughest exams - The Knowledge of London. Each candidate must memorise the capital's 25,000 streets, traffic restrictions and ever-changing points of interest, which takes an average of four years of determination to successfully pass

programme.title: Danger Man
programme.desc: John Drake (Patrick McGoohan), a secret agent working for NATO, travels to different corners of the world in order to prevent crimes that threaten world peace

programme.title: The Old Guys
programme.desc: Tom and Roy take up Sally's offer of a week in a Highland croft, where she was going to stay with her boyfriend Mark before his death. While Roy quickly adapts to country life, Tom is soon bored and invites a recently dumped Amber to join them. However, a farcical situation ensues with the arrival of a couple of unexpected visitors

programme.title: The Bionic Vet
programme.desc: A cross-border collie suffers a setback when a disc from its spine blows and Noel Fitzpatrick designs a special bolt to substitute it. A four-year-old cat is taken to the surgery after being hit by a car, and it is bad news for the owners when the X-rays reveal its pelvis is badly damaged and the operation may cost thousands of pounds

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring and revealing Brixton and Harmondsworth for locals and tourists. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.title: Rachel Khoo's Kitchen Notebook
programme.desc: The Paris-based chef returns to her home city to see what its most interesting eateries have to offer. In the first edition, she visits a pop-up restaurant in a boutique

programme.title: Rachel Khoo's Kitchen Notebook
programme.desc: The chef creates simple and colourful canapes, before making a sophisticated jelly for grown-ups

programme.title: All At Sea (1970)
programme.desc: A boy travelling on a ship intends to deliver a package to his pen pal in Tangiers, but the parcel goes missing after being falsely identified as contraband. Family drama, starring Gary Smith and Stephen Mallett

programme.title: Kind Hearts and Coronets (1949)
programme.desc: The penniless grandson of a duke plots to eliminate the eight relatives standing between him and a vast inheritance. He shoots, drowns, poisons and explodes his way through his family tree, but his final triumph is ruined when his lady love's husband kills himself. Ealing black comedy, starring Alec Guinness as all eight potential victims, with Dennis Price, Valerie Hobson and Joan Greenwood

programme.title: Henry V (1989)
programme.desc: The English king leads his armies into war to reclaim control of France - culminating in a pivotal confrontation with much larger enemy forces on the fields of Agincourt. Adaptation of Shakespeare's historical play, directed by and starring Kenneth Branagh. With Derek Jacobi, Brian Blessed, Emma Thompson, Ian Holm, Robert Stephens and a young Christian Bale

programme.title: Discover London
programme.desc: Explore hidden gems and alternative areas of the capital, learning more about the cultural history of one of the world's greatest cities

programme.title: My Greek Odyssey
programme.desc: Peter Maneas embarks on a journey through some of the most spectacular and unique Greek islands, exploring the rich culture, history and food of Greece while meeting the locals and those who live there

programme.title: Stella Street
programme.desc: Mick decides to dedicate his life to religion, while Joe receives decorating advice from Roger Moore. Comedy, written and performed by John Sessions and Phil Cornwell

programme.title: Stella Street
programme.desc: Mick's preaching starts to annoy the neighbours and Dirk Bogarde reveals some information to the police. Comedy, written and performed by John Sessions and Phil Cornwell

programme.title: Stella Street
programme.desc: A drunk John Hurt arrives in the neighbourhood, while Joe takes advantage of Michael's absence by taking over his house. The lives, loves and laundry of the famous stars living in leafy Surbiton, as performed by impressionists John Sessions and Phil Cornwell

programme.title: A Perfect 14 ()
programme.desc: The fascinating world of plus-size modelling and the women involved who are fighting to reshape fashion and the standards of society

programme.title: Desperate Characters (1971)
programme.desc: A burglary turns a middle-aged couple's life together upside down, and makes them question the fundamental values they've always believed in. Drama, starring Shirley MacLaine, Kenneth Mars, Gerald O'Loughlin and Sada Thompson

programme.title: Jailhouse Wardress (1981)
programme.desc: A female Nazi prison warden goes into hiding in South America after the Second World War. She oversees a secret facility where genetically engineered sex slaves are created for her fellow war criminals, while also plotting revenge for the Third Reich's defeat. Drama, starring Monica Swinn and Michael Bates

programme.title: All Coppers Are (1971)

programme.desc: The Tabernacle Choir sing classical greats in this series of special Easter performances of music and spoken word

programme.desc: The Tabernacle Choir sing classical greats in this series of special Easter performances of music and spoken word

programme.title: The Knowledge: The World's Toughest Taxi Test
programme.desc: A behind-the-scenes look at the test that all black cab drivers working in the city of London must take, one of the world's toughest exams - The Knowledge of London. Each candidate must memorise the capital's 25,000 streets, traffic restrictions and ever-changing points of interest, which takes an average of four years of determination to successfully pass

programme.title: Best of The Tube
programme.desc: Recapping the finest moments from the music show that was hosted by Jools Holland and the late Paula Yates

programme.title: Best of The Tube
programme.desc: Recapping the finest moments from the music show that was hosted by Jools Holland and the late Paula Yates

programme.title: Manganinnie (1980)
programme.desc: An Australian Aboriginal woman goes in search of her lost tribe, accompanied on her journey by a lost white girl. with whom she forms a close bond despite having no common language. As their journey towards the coast continues, the woman begins to suspect her people and culture may have been all but wiped out by British colonists. Historical drama, starring Mawuyul Yanthalawuy and Anna Ralph

programme.title: We Dive at Dawn (1943)
programme.desc: A British submarine crew is unexpectedly ordered to carry out a top-secret mission to sink the German fleet's latest battleship in the Baltic Sea. But it is not long before disaster strikes and the vessel becomes stranded in the middle of the ocean with no fuel or food. Second World War adventure, starring John Mills, Eric Portman, Reginald Purdell and Niall MacGinnis

programme.title: Bloomfield (1970)
programme.desc: An Israeli footballer nearing the end of his career is tempted to accept a bribe to throw his final game. A devoted young fan and a woman who is in love with him prick his conscience, but even if he chooses to do the right thing, his failing skills may not be enough to triumph over younger, fitter opposition. Sporting drama based on Joseph Gross' novel, co-directed by and starring Richard Harris, with Romy Schneider and Kim Burfield

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours narrated by Dewyne Lindsay, this time exploring Canary Wharf and Southbank.

programme.title: A Year at Kew
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh, a student at Kew in the 1970s, offers an insight into the work carried out behind the scenes by the 150 gardening staff at the Royal Botanic Gardens. Palm House keeper Emma Fox is seen doing battle with a plague of thrips, while tree gang member Jon Hammerton fells a diseased tree

programme.title: A Year at Kew
programme.desc: Expert in foul odours Dr Geoffrey Kite eagerly anticipates the rare flowering of the mighty Titan arum, an evil-smelling bloom lasting just 24 hours. Simon and Tim Cole work against the clock to complete the badger sett and field hospital in time for Kew's Go Wild festival

programme.title: My Greek Odyssey
programme.desc: Peter Maneas embarks on a journey through some of the most spectacular and unique Greek islands, exploring the rich culture, history and food of Greece while meeting the locals and those who live there

programme.title: Queens
programme.desc: 16th-century Europe was a hotbed of tense international intrigue. Mary Stuart and Elizabeth I confront one another on matters religious, political and personal

programme.title: The Hatton Garden Job (2017)
programme.desc: A ragtag group of career criminals plot an audacious, multi-million-pound raid on an underground facility in London. True-life crime drama, with Stephen Moyer, Larry Lamb and Matthew Goode

programme.title: Operation Chromite (2016)
programme.desc: A squad of South Korean soldiers sent to infiltrate their northern neighbours during the crucial Battle of Incheon. Wartime drama, starring Lee Jung-jae and Liam Neeson

programme.title: Eugenie De Sade (1973)
programme.desc: A teenage girl lives alone with her stepfather after the death of her mother, and becomes fascinated with the dark erotic stories he writes. As she grows more and more sexually obsessed with him and his tales, she fails to realise the dangerous secrets that have inspired his creations. Erotic thriller, starring Soledad Miranda and Paul Muller

programme.title: Stella Street
programme.desc: Mick decides to dedicate his life to religion, while Joe receives decorating advice from Roger Moore. Comedy, written and performed by John Sessions and Phil Cornwell

programme.title: Skid Kids (1953)
programme.desc: A group of kids takes on a gang of bike thieves, with a little help from the police. Children's drama, starring Barry MacGregor and Anthony Lang

programme.title: Henry V (1989)
programme.desc: The English king leads his armies into war to reclaim control of France - culminating in a pivotal confrontation with much larger enemy forces on the fields of Agincourt. Adaptation of Shakespeare's historical play, directed by and starring Kenneth Branagh. With Derek Jacobi, Brian Blessed, Emma Thompson, Ian Holm, Robert Stephens and a young Christian Bale

programme.title: The Likely Lads (1976)
programme.desc: Bob and Thelma embark on a chaotic caravan holiday - with best pal Terry and a glamorous Scandinavian au pair in tow, ensuring that nothing will go according to plan. Comedy based on the TV series, starring James Bolam, Rodney Bewes, Brigit Forsyth and Mary Tamm

programme.title: One Good Turn (1954)
programme.desc: A boy living in an orphanage grows up to be the institution's lovable but clumsy caretaker. He must save the children's home from a wicked property developer who wants to build a factory on the site, and tricks him into taking the kids to the seaside so he can knock the building down. Comedy, starring Norman Wisdom and Joan Rice

programme.title: The Fast Lady (1963)
programme.desc: A cycling enthusiast on a modest civil servant's salary gives up his beloved bike to a buy a luxury car to impress a local magistrate's daughter. However, he first has to pass his driving test to stand any chance of winning her heart - and in the process ends up in a wild car-chase after a gang of robbers. Comedy, with Julie Christie, Stanley Baxter, James Robertson Justice, Leslie Phillips and Dick Emery

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring Forest Hill, Hither Green and Cubitt Town. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.title: Killing Hitler
programme.desc: Part one of two. A blend of documentary, dramatic reconstruction and expert military analysis brings to life the blueprint of Operation Foxley - a 1944 plan to assassinate Adolf Hitler. Featuring testimonies from operatives of Churchill's Special Operations Executive and one of the Fuehrer's most trusted bodyguards, the ethical right to kill an enemy head of state is debated during the unfolding story of LB/X - the man in charge of the plot to kill the Allies' most hated foe

programme.title: The Comic Strip Presents
programme.desc: Two inept hitmen pursue a banker who has embezzled from the Mafia through the streets of London - but have no clue what to do with him when they catch him

programme.title: Stella Street
programme.desc: Mick decides to dedicate his life to religion, while Joe receives decorating advice from Roger Moore. Comedy, written and performed by John Sessions and Phil Cornwell

programme.title: Escape from Sobibor (1987)
programme.desc: New arrivals at a Nazi concentration camp realise they do not have long to live as the Nazi campaign of genocide gathers speed. The commandant threatens to execute one prisoner for every escape attempt - leading the inmates to realise that the only hope is for all 600 of them to break out together. Drama based on the true story of the largest uprising during the Holocaust, starring Alan Arkin, Joanna Pacula and Rutger Hauer

programme.title: Urban Hymn (2015)
programme.desc: A teenager is encouraged by her social worker to use singing as an escape route from her troubled personal life. She soon finds her loyalties divided between the fresh start music provides for her and the influence of her possessive and volatile best friend. Drama, starring Letitia Wright, Shirley Henderson and Ian Hart

programme.title: Stella Street
programme.desc: Mick's preaching starts to annoy the neighbours and Dirk Bogarde reveals some information to the police. Comedy, written and performed by John Sessions and Phil Cornwell

programme.title: A London Community in Lockdown
programme.desc: A heartfelt look at life during lockdown in the Listraban community in London, a record of a moment in time that will be in the history books for generations to come

programme.title: All At Sea (1970)

programme.title: Worlds Apart with Oksana Boyko
programme.desc: Worlds Apart is a fast-paced, in-depth discussion on the pressing issues of the day. The host Oksana Boyko strives to depart from the traditional Q-and-A form of interview in favor of an emotive and engaging conversation. While the title and format of the program presuppose differences of opinion, WA's main goal is to facilitate public debate in an intelligent and respectful manner

programme.title: Live Betfred Super League
programme.desc: Hull Kingston Rovers v St Helens (Kick-off 8.15pm). Coverage of the match from the second round of fixtures, which takes place at Totally Wicked Stadium. The last meeting between the two sides took place in September 2020, when St Helens score a last-minute drop-goal to win a tight encounter 21-20. Theo Fages made the golden-point kick to extended the Saints unbeaten run to six games on their way to winning the 2020 Grand Final.

programme.desc: Hull Kingston Rovers v St Helens (Kick-off 8.15pm). Coverage of the match from the second round of fixtures, which takes place at Totally Wicked Stadium. The last meeting between the two sides took place in September 2020, when St Helens score a last-minute drop-goal to win a tight encounter 21-20.
